Numeric priority ranking (1 = highest priority) for evacuating this zone under this specific plan. Priority may differ across plans — a VIP hospitality zone may be priority 1 under an active shooter plan but priority 3 under a severe weather plan.

Narrative description of primary and secondary evacuation routes for this plan, including ADA-accessible routes, assembly points, and crowd flow management corridors. Aligned with venue floor plan and ADA compliance requirements. [Moved from emergency_response_plan: This STRING field in emergency_response_plan is a denormalized narrative that attempts to capture zone-level evacuation routing. The proper model is to designate specific access_zone records with evacuation_priority_level and zone_role_in_plan in the plan_zone_designation association, replacing this free-text field with structured, queryable zone designations. The narrative description, if needed, can be retained as a notes field on the designation record.]

description: Calendar date on which this zone designation expires or is superseded within the plan. Null indicates the designation remains active. Enables historical tracking of which zones served which roles under which plan versions.

Indicates whether this access zone is designated as a shelter-in-place location under this specific emergency response plan. Belongs to the designation relationship because the same zone may be a shelter-in-place zone under a severe weather plan but an evacuation zone under a fire plan.

Indicates whether this access zone is designated as a medical triage area under this specific emergency response plan. A zone may be a triage zone under an MCI plan but not under a fire evacuation plan — hence this flag belongs to the designation, not the zone master record.

The specific operational role this access zone is designated to perform under this emergency response plan. A zone may serve as a triage area under one plan and an evacuation staging area under another. This attribute belongs to the designation relationship, not to either the plan or the zone alone.

description: Timestamp at which the fan's most recent consent preferences were recorded or last updated. Used as the audit timestamp for GDPR and CCPA consent records. Required for demonstrating lawful basis of processing under GDPR Article 7.
reference: GDPR Article 7; CCPA §1798.120; Salesforce CRM Privacy Center

description: Timestamp at which the fan account record was first created in the source system (Salesforce CRM). Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and registration cohort analysis.
reference: Salesforce CRM CreatedDate; GDPR Article 5(1)(e); ISO 8601

description: Externally-known alphanumeric account number assigned by Salesforce CRM. Used as the business-facing identifier on correspondence, ticketing portals, and fan-facing digital platforms. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_AGREEMENT entity.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Object; Internal Account Numbering Standard

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the fan's preferred transaction currency (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, AUD). Used for pricing display, ticketing transactions, and merchandise purchases on the fan's account.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA SD Currency Configuration; Salesforce CRM

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the jurisdiction in which the fan's personal data must be stored and processed. Drives data residency enforcement, GDPR adequacy decisions, and cross-border transfer controls.
reference: GDPR Chapter V (International Transfers); ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; CCPA

description: Indicates whether the stored payment instrument is designated as the default payment method for this account. True when this is the primary instrument used for automated renewals, loyalty redemptions, and one-click purchases.
reference: Salesforce CRM Payment Methods; PCI-DSS v4.0

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Consent Flag
description: Indicates whether the fan has provided explicit GDPR consent for personal data processing. True when valid consent has been recorded. Mandatory for fan accounts in EU jurisdictions. Drives data processing eligibility and marketing suppression lists.
reference: GDPR Article 6(1)(a); GDPR Article 7; Salesforce CRM Consent Management

description: BCP 47 language tag representing the fan's preferred language for communications and digital platform content (e.g., en-US, es-MX, fr-FR, pt-BR). Used for localized content delivery, email template selection, and multilingual customer service routing.
reference: BCP 47 (IETF RFC 5646); GDPR Article 12 (Transparent Communication); Salesforce CRM

description: Timestamp of the most recent successful authentication event on the fan account. Used for dormancy detection, re-engagement campaign targeting, and security anomaly detection. Sourced from Salesforce CRM or Adobe Experience Platform session logs.
reference: Salesforce CRM Login History; Adobe Experience Platform; GDPR Article 5(1)(e) (Storage Limitation)

description: Number of consecutive failed login attempts since the last successful authentication. Used to trigger account lockout policies per security standards. Resets to zero upon successful login. Sourced from Salesforce CRM identity management.
reference: PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 8.3; NIST SP 800-63B; Salesforce CRM

description: Current unredeemed loyalty points balance on the fan account at the time of the last update. Raw operational balance used for redemption eligibility checks and tier qualification. Not a calculated aggregate — sourced directly from the CRM loyalty ledger.
reference: Salesforce CRM Loyalty Management; Sports Entertainment Loyalty Program Policy

description: Total loyalty points earned by the fan account in the current calendar or fiscal year. Used for annual tier qualification thresholds and year-end loyalty reporting. Resets at the start of each program year.
reference: Sports Entertainment Loyalty Program Policy; Salesforce CRM Loyalty Management

description: Indicates whether the fan has opted in to receive marketing and promotional email communications. True when explicit opt-in consent has been recorded. Used by CRM to control email campaign eligibility and suppression lists.
reference: GDPR Article 6(1)(a); CAN-SPAM Act; CASL; Salesforce CRM Marketing Cloud

description: Unique alphanumeric membership identifier printed on physical or digital membership cards. Used for in-venue access control, loyalty redemption at Point of Sale (POS), and fan-facing account lookup. Distinct from crm_account_number — this is the fan-visible card number.
reference: Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management; Ticketmaster/AXS Access Control

description: Indicates whether Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) is currently enabled on the fan account. True when the fan has enrolled a second authentication factor (e.g., SMS OTP, authenticator app). Used for security compliance reporting and risk-based access control.
reference: NIST SP 800-63B; PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 8; Salesforce CRM Identity

description: Most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) recorded for this fan account, on a scale of 0–10. Collected via post-event or periodic surveys. Used for fan satisfaction tracking, churn prediction, and experience improvement initiatives. Raw survey response — not a calculated aggregate.
reference: NPS Methodology (Bain & Company); Adobe Experience Platform Survey; Sports Entertainment Fan Experience KPI Framework

description: Date on which the most recent NPS survey response was recorded for this fan account. Used to determine survey recency, control survey frequency, and align NPS scores with event or season context.
reference: NPS Methodology (Bain & Company); Adobe Experience Platform; Sports Entertainment Fan Experience KPI Framework

description: Date on which the fan last changed their account password. Used for security policy enforcement, forced password rotation reminders, and breach response workflows. Does not store the password itself — credential metadata only.
reference: Salesforce CRM Identity; NIST SP 800-63B; PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 8

description: PCI-DSS compliant tokenized representation of the fan's default payment instrument on file. The token is issued by the payment processor and replaces the actual card number (PAN). Never stores raw card data. Used for recurring purchases, loyalty redemptions, and ticketing transactions.

description: Primary email address associated with the fan account, used for login authentication, transactional notifications, and marketing communications. Subject to GDPR and CCPA consent requirements. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Account email field.

description: Indicates whether the fan has enabled push notifications on the mobile app. True when the fan has granted notification permissions. Used for real-time event alerts, score updates, and personalized offers via Adobe Experience Platform.

Used for channel attribution and acquisition cost analysis.

description: Indicates whether the fan account holds at least one active season ticket package for the current season. True when one or more season ticket entitlements are linked to this account. Used for priority access, renewal campaigns, and season ticket holder benefit eligibility.

description: Indicates whether the fan has opted in to receive SMS/text message marketing communications. True when explicit opt-in consent has been recorded. Required for TCPA compliance in the US and equivalent regulations in other jurisdictions.

description: Indicates whether the fan account is designated as a Very Important Person (VIP), qualifying for premium hospitality, dedicated concierge services, and exclusive event access. Set by CRM administrators based on spend thresholds or manual designation.

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: High-level category grouping for this consent type, aligned with IAB TCF (Transparency and Consent Framework) and cookie consent banner classifications. Supports consent management platform (CMP) integration and reporting.

description: Timestamp at which the fan actively granted this consent. Represents the principal business event timestamp for the consent lifecycle. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.

description: ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) of the language in which the consent notice was presented to the fan. Ensures consent was given in the fan's understood language, a GDPR requirement.

description: URL of the specific privacy notice or consent form presented to the fan at the time of consent capture. Provides an auditable link to the exact disclosure document the fan reviewed.

description: Reference identifier or URL pointing to the stored evidence of consent (e.g., a screenshot, form submission log, call recording reference, or digital audit token). Required for demonstrating consent under GDPR Article 7(1).

description: Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to this consent record, used for audit trails, regulatory inquiries, and fan-facing acknowledgement receipts. Generated at point of consent capture.

description: Date on which a consent renewal reminder should be sent to the fan, typically triggered before the effective_until date. Supports proactive consent lifecycle management to avoid consent lapse.

description: The channel or touchpoint through which the fan provided or updated their consent. Used for audit purposes and to validate that consent was obtained through an appropriate mechanism. Values: web_form, mobile_app, in_venue_kiosk, call_center, paper_form, third_party.
reference: GDPR Article 7(1); CCPA Section 1798.135; Adobe Experience Platform Consent Schema

description: Current lifecycle state of this consent record. 'granted' indicates active consent; 'withdrawn' indicates the fan has revoked consent; 'pending' indicates consent request issued but not yet confirmed; 'expired' indicates consent has lapsed past its validity period.
reference: GDPR Article 7(3); CCPA Section 1798.120(b); Adobe Experience Platform Consent Schema

description: Classification of the specific data processing activity for which consent is being captured. Covers marketing email, SMS, push notification, data sharing with partners, profiling/personalization, and cookie consent. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: marketing_email|sms|push_notification|data_sharing_partner|profiling_personalization|cookie — promote to reference product if additional types are required]

description: Version identifier of the privacy policy, terms of service, or consent notice document that the fan agreed to at the time of consent. Enables tracking of consent against specific policy versions for regulatory compliance.

description: Timestamp at which this consent preference record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and regulatory record-keeping under GDPR Article 30.

description: Type of device used by the fan when submitting their consent. Supports channel analytics and ensures consent UI was appropriate for the device used.

description: Timestamp at which the fan completed the double opt-in confirmation step. Null if double opt-in was not required or not completed. Supports audit evidence for email marketing consent.

description: Date from which this consent record is binding and processing under this consent is permitted. Supports forward-dated consent activations and consent version transitions.

description: Date on which this consent record expires and processing must cease unless renewed. Null for open-ended consents with no defined expiry. Supports consent validity period management.

description: IP address of the device from which the fan submitted their consent. Used as supporting evidence of consent origin and geographic jurisdiction determination. May be considered PII under GDPR.

description: The regulatory framework and geographic jurisdiction under which this consent record is governed. Determines which privacy law obligations apply. description: The GDPR Article 6 lawful basis under which personal data is processed for this consent record. While this product primarily captures consent, the legal basis field supports cases where processing may shift to another basis (e.g., contract fulfilment). Values: consent, legitimate_interest, contract, legal_obligation, vital_interest, public_task.

description: Indicates whether the fan is a minor (under 16 in EU, under 13 in US) at the time of consent. Triggers parental consent verification workflows and restricts certain processing activities under GDPR Article 8 and COPPA.

description: Indicates whether the fan has actively opted in (True) or opted out (False) for this consent type. Provides a direct boolean signal for downstream marketing suppression lists and personalisation engines.

description: Detailed description of the specific data processing purpose for which consent is granted, as disclosed to the fan. Examples include 'personalised match recommendations', 'third-party sponsor marketing', 'fan behaviour analytics'. Supports GDPR purpose limitation principle.

description: Indicates whether the fan has submitted a Right to Erasure (Right to be Forgotten) request under GDPR Article 17 or CCPA deletion request. Triggers downstream data deletion workflows across all processing systems.

description: Indicates whether this payment instrument is authorized by the fan for automatic recurring charges such as season ticket renewals, membership subscriptions, and OTT streaming plan renewals. Requires explicit fan consent per GDPR and CCPA mandates.
reference: GDPR Article 6 (Lawful Basis for Processing); CCPA §1798.120; FTC Negative Option Rule

description: Response code returned by the card issuer's Address Verification Service (AVS) during the enrollment authorization. Indicates the degree of match between the billing address provided by the fan and the address on file with the issuer. Used in fraud scoring and risk decisioning.
reference: Visa AVS Response Codes; Mastercard AVS Response Codes; PCI DSS v4.0

description: Last four digits of the bank account number for direct debit payment methods. Displayed to the fan for identification purposes. Applicable only when payment_method_type is 'direct_debit'. Null for card-based instruments.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0; NACHA ACH Rules; SEPA Direct Debit Scheme

description: Bank routing number (US ACH) or sort code (UK/EU) associated with a direct debit payment method. Used for ACH or SEPA payment processing. Stored in truncated or tokenized form. Null for non-direct-debit instruments.
reference: NACHA ACH Operating Rules; SEPA Direct Debit Core Scheme Rulebook

description: Primary street address line of the billing address associated with this payment instrument. Used for Address Verification Service (AVS) checks during card-not-present transactions on ticketing and merchandise platforms.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 12.3; GDPR Article 4; CCPA §1798.140

description: Secondary street address line (apartment, suite, unit number) of the billing address. Optional supplementary address detail for Address Verification Service (AVS) processing.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0; GDPR Article 4; CCPA §1798.140

description: City component of the billing address associated with this payment instrument. Used for AVS verification, fraud scoring, and geographic revenue reporting.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0; GDPR Article 4; CCPA §1798.140

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the billing address (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Determines applicable privacy regulation (GDPR for EU/EEA, CCPA for California), currency routing, and cross-border payment processing rules.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR Article 3; CCPA §1798.140; PCI DSS v4.0

description: Postal or ZIP code of the billing address. Critical input for Address Verification Service (AVS) checks on card-not-present transactions. Also used for geographic segmentation and tax jurisdiction mapping.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0; GDPR Article 4; CCPA §1798.140

description: State or province component of the billing address. Used for AVS verification, tax jurisdiction determination, and CCPA/GDPR applicability assessment for fan data privacy compliance.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0; CCPA §1798.140; GDPR Article 4

business_glossary_term: Bank Identification Number (BIN) / Issuer Identification Number (IIN)
description: First 6–8 digits of the payment card number identifying the issuing institution and card product type. Used for routing decisions, interchange category determination, and fraud analysis. Stored as a non-sensitive BIN (not the full PAN) per PCI-DSS truncation rules.
reference: ISO 7812 BIN/IIN Standard; PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3.3.1

description: Payment network or card scheme brand associated with the instrument (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, Amex). Applicable for credit and debit card types. Null for non-card payment methods such as digital wallets or direct debit. Used for interchange fee analysis and brand-level reporting.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0; Visa/Mastercard Network Rules

description: Funding classification of the payment card as determined from BIN lookup. Distinguishes credit, debit, prepaid, and charge cards. Impacts interchange fee rates, refund processing timelines, and eligibility for certain promotional financing offers on season tickets.
reference: Visa/Mastercard BIN Database; ISO 7812; PCI DSS v4.0

description: Last four digits of the payment card Primary Account Number (PAN). Displayed to the fan in account management and checkout screens to identify their saved card without exposing the full PAN. Permitted display element under PCI-DSS truncation rules.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3.3.1; PCI SSC Truncation Guidelines

description: Name of the individual as it appears on the payment card or as registered with the payment instrument. Used for display in account management screens and for AVS/identity verification during high-value transactions. Stored only where operationally required per PCI-DSS data minimization principles.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3.3; GDPR Article 5 (Data Minimisation); CCPA §1798.140

description: Date and time when the fan explicitly consented to storing this payment instrument and authorizing recurring charges. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Required for GDPR Article 6 lawful basis documentation and CCPA compliance audit trails.
reference: GDPR Article 6 and Article 7; CCPA §1798.120; PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 12.3

description: Version identifier of the terms and conditions or privacy policy under which the fan consented to storing this payment instrument. Enables compliance teams to identify fans who consented under outdated policy versions requiring re-consent.
reference: GDPR Article 7; CCPA §1798.120; Adobe Experience Platform Consent Management

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code associated with the payment instrument (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency fan accounts across global markets. Relevant for gift card balances, direct debit mandates, and cross-border transaction routing.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; PCI DSS v4.0

description: Unique mandate reference number assigned for SEPA Direct Debit or ACH authorization agreements. Required for recurring direct debit collections such as installment-based season ticket payment plans. Null for non-direct-debit instruments.
reference: SEPA Direct Debit Core Scheme Rulebook; NACHA ACH Operating Rules; PCI DSS v4.0

description: Channel through which the fan added this payment instrument to their account. Supports fraud analysis, channel attribution, and UX optimization. 'pos' — Point of Sale terminal at venue; 'partner_api' — enrolled via third-party integration (e.g., Ticketmaster, Shopify Plus).
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Channel Attribution; Ticketmaster/AXS Integration

description: Date and time when the payment instrument was first added to the fan account. Used for lifecycle management, fraud velocity checks, and compliance audit trails. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10; GDPR Article 30 (Records of Processing)

description: Two-digit calendar month (1–12) in which the payment card expires. Used in conjunction with expiry_year to determine card validity and trigger pre-expiry notification workflows for membership auto-renewal continuity.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3.3; ISO 8583 Card Data Standards

description: Indicates whether a pre-expiry notification has been dispatched to the fan via their preferred communication channel (email, SMS, push) to prompt card update before membership or season ticket auto-renewal fails. Managed by Adobe Experience Platform journey automation.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Journey Optimizer; Salesforce CRM Marketing Cloud

description: Four-digit calendar year in which the payment card expires. Combined with expiry_month to assess card validity. Triggers automated pre-expiry fan communications to update payment details and prevent lapsed season ticket or membership renewals.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3.3; ISO 8583 Card Data Standards

description: Numeric risk score (0.00–100.00) assigned to this payment instrument by the fraud detection engine at enrollment or last transaction. Higher scores indicate elevated fraud risk. Used to trigger step-up authentication, manual review, or instrument suspension. Not a calculated KPI — raw score from fraud provider.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 12.3; Adyen RevenueProtect; Stripe Radar

description: Current monetary balance remaining on a stored-value gift card payment instrument. Applicable only when payment_method_type is 'gift_card'. Updated after each redemption transaction. Null for non-gift-card instruments. Denominated in the currency indicated by currency_code.
reference: Shopify Plus Gift Card API; PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3

description: Indicates whether this payment instrument is the fan's designated default method for one-click purchases, membership auto-renewals, and express checkout. Only one payment method per fan account should have this flag set to True at any time.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Fan Account Preferences; Shopify Plus Customer Payment Methods API

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the financial institution that issued the payment card. Derived from the card BIN (Bank Identification Number). Used for cross-border transaction fee assessment, currency mismatch detection, and regulatory reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; ISO 7812 BIN/IIN Standard; PCI DSS v4.0

description: Date and time when this payment method record was most recently modified (e.g., billing address update, status change, token refresh). Supports audit trail requirements and Silver Layer data lineage tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10; GDPR Article 30; SOX Section 404

description: Date and time of the most recent successful transaction processed against this payment instrument. Used to identify dormant payment methods for cleanup workflows and to assess instrument activity for fraud monitoring.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 12.3; Ticketmaster/AXS Transaction Logs

description: Current lifecycle status of the payment instrument. 'active' — valid and available for transactions; 'expired' — card expiry date has passed; 'suspended' — temporarily blocked due to fraud or dispute; 'removed' — fan has deleted the instrument; 'pending_verification' — awaiting micro-deposit or 3DS verification.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 12.3; Ticketmaster/AXS Payment Vault

description: Unique identifier assigned by the card network (Visa, Mastercard) during the initial card-on-file enrollment authorization. Required for subsequent MIT (Merchant-Initiated Transaction) recurring charges such as season ticket auto-renewals, ensuring compliance with card network stored credential mandates.
reference: Visa Stored Credential Framework; Mastercard Stored Credential Transaction Framework; PCI DSS v4.0

description: Classification of the payment instrument category. Drives checkout flow logic, fee structures, and refund eligibility across ticketing (Ticketmaster/AXS), merchandise (Shopify Plus), and membership auto-renewal workflows.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 12.3; ISO 20022 Payment Instrument Classification

business_glossary_term: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI-DSS) Compliance Status
description: Indicates the PCI-DSS compliance posture of this payment instrument record. 'compliant' — token and data handling meet PCI-DSS v4.0 requirements; 'non_compliant' — a compliance gap has been identified requiring remediation; 'pending_review' — under compliance assessment. Supports PCI SSC audit reporting.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0; PCI SSC Tokenization Guidelines

description: External reference identifier for this payment token as recorded in the originating operational system (e.g., Ticketmaster/AXS vault ID, Shopify Plus payment method ID, Salesforce CRM payment record ID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and lineage tracing in the Silver Layer.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Payment Vault; Shopify Plus Payment Methods API; Salesforce CRM

description: Opaque tokenized representation of the underlying payment instrument issued by the tokenization provider (e.g., Braintree, Stripe, Adyen). Replaces the raw PAN in all downstream systems. Never stores the actual card number. Compliant with PCI-DSS tokenization standards.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3.5; PCI SSC Tokenization Guidelines

description: Name of the third-party PCI-DSS compliant tokenization service provider that issued and manages the payment token. Used for routing, reconciliation, and vendor management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Braintree|Adyen|Stripe|CyberSource|Worldpay|Checkout.com|Spreedly — promote to reference product if provider list grows beyond 6]
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3.5; PCI SSC Tokenization Guidelines

description: Method used to verify the payment instrument at enrollment. '3ds' — 3-D Secure authentication (Visa Secure / Mastercard Identity Check); 'avs' — Address Verification Service; 'micro_deposit' — two small deposits for bank account verification; 'cvv_check' — card security code validation; 'none' — no additional verification performed.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 8; EMVCo 3-D Secure Specification; NACHA ACH Rules

description: Outcome of the payment instrument verification process. 'verified' — successfully authenticated; 'unverified' — verification not yet completed; 'failed' — verification attempt was unsuccessful; 'pending' — verification in progress. Determines eligibility for high-value transactions such as season ticket purchases.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 8; EMVCo 3-D Secure Specification

description: Name of the digital wallet service when payment_method_type is 'digital_wallet'. Identifies the wallet ecosystem for routing, reconciliation, and fan experience analytics. Null for non-wallet payment types.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0; EMVCo Tokenization Specification

description: Primary key for segment

Foreign key linking to compliance.data_processing_record. Business justification: Fan segmentation involves profiling personal data, which must be documented as a processing activity under GDPR Article 30. A DPO managing ROPA compliance would expect each segment definition to refer
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Identifier of the specific team or franchise associated with this segment. Enables team-level fan segmentation for localized marketing, merchandise promotions, and event-specific campaigns.
reference: Salesforce CRM Team Account Object; Internal Team Master Data

description: Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Fan segments are scoped to a specific league for targeted marketing and broadcast rights compliance. The existing target_league is a denormalized text field; replacing it with a proper FK to league.le
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Fan segments are frequently season-scoped (e.g., 'Current season STH', '2024 lapsed ticket buyers'). Season-specific segment refresh schedules and campaign targeting require a direct FK to league seas
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to content.platform_channel. Business justification: Segment-targeted content distribution: sports entertainment DTC/OTT operations assign fan segments to specific platform channels for personalized content delivery. A domain expert expects this link fo
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.policy. Business justification: Audience segmentation using personal data must be authorized by a specific compliance policy (GDPR lawful basis, CCPA). The segment product has consent_requirement and gdpr_applicable — a DPO would ex
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Native identifier of this segment in the originating source system (e.g., Adobe Experience Platform audience GUID or Salesforce CRM campaign segment ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational CRM/CDP systems.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Segment API; Salesforce CRM Campaign Object

description: Identifier of the specific team or franchise associated with this segment. Enables team-level fan segmentation for localized marketing, merchandise promotions, and event-specific campaigns.
reference: Salesforce CRM Team Account Object; Internal Team Master Data

description: Maximum age (in years) of fans included in this segment. Used in conjunction with age_band_min to define a demographic age range for targeted campaigns. Nullable if no upper age bound is defined.
reference: Internal Fan Demographic Policy; Adobe Experience Platform Audience Attributes

description: Minimum age (in years) of fans included in this segment when demographic segmentation is applied. Used for age-gated content, alcohol sponsorship campaigns, and youth-specific engagement programs. Supports ADA and child privacy compliance.
reference: COPPA (Children's Online Privacy Protection Act); ADA; Internal Fan Demographic Policy

business_glossary_term: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) Applicable Flag
description: Indicates whether this segment includes California residents subject to CCPA regulation. When true, opt-out of sale rights and data disclosure requirements must be honored before campaign activation.
reference: CCPA Section 1798.100; CCPA Section 1798.120

description: Comma-separated list of marketing and engagement channels for which this segment is activated (e.g., 'email, push_notification, sms, in_app, paid_social, display'). Guides campaign execution teams on appropriate activation channels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|push_notification|sms|in_app|paid_social|display|direct_mail — promote to reference product]
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Destinations; Salesforce Marketing Cloud Channel Configuration

description: Specifies the consent basis required for fans to be included in this segment and activated for marketing. Explicit consent is required for GDPR-regulated EU fans; legitimate interest may apply for certain analytics use cases. Ensures compliance with GDPR and CCPA.
reference: GDPR Article 6 (Lawful Basis); GDPR Article 7 (Consent); CCPA Section 1798.100

description: Date and time when this segment record was first created in the source system and ingested into the Silver Layer. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Provides audit trail for segment lifecycle management.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability)

description: Structured or semi-structured expression of the business rules and logic used to qualify fans into this segment (e.g., SQL predicate, JSON rule set, or natural-language description of inclusion/exclusion criteria). Sourced from Adobe Experience Platform segment builder or Salesforce CRM campaign filter logic.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Segment Builder; Salesforce CRM Campaign Criteria

description: Format of the definition_criteria field, indicating how the segment logic is expressed. PQL refers to Adobe Experience Platform Profile Query Language. Enables downstream systems to parse and execute the segment definition correctly.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Profile Query Language (PQL) Specification

description: Date from which this segment definition is considered active and eligible for campaign use. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd. Supports time-bounded segmentation strategies such as seasonal campaigns or event-specific audiences.
reference: Salesforce CRM Campaign Start Date; Adobe Experience Platform Segment Validity

description: Date after which this segment definition expires and is no longer eligible for active campaign targeting. Nullable for evergreen segments with no defined end date. Formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: Salesforce CRM Campaign End Date; Adobe Experience Platform Segment Validity

description: Maximum fan engagement score for inclusion in this segment. Used in conjunction with engagement_score_min to define a score band for precise audience targeting. Nullable if no upper bound is defined.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Fan Engagement Scoring

description: Minimum fan engagement score required for inclusion in this segment, as calculated by Adobe Experience Platform. Engagement scores reflect fan interaction frequency, recency, and depth across digital and physical touchpoints.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Fan Engagement Scoring

description: Number of fan profiles qualifying for this segment as of the last refresh date. Represents the segment size used for campaign reach estimation, budget planning, and audience reporting. Not a real-time count — reflects the most recent batch evaluation.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Audience Size Metric; Salesforce CRM Campaign Member Count

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Applicable Flag
description: Indicates whether this segment includes fans subject to GDPR regulation (EU/EEA residents). When true, additional consent validation and data processing restrictions apply before campaign activation.
reference: GDPR Article 3 (Territorial Scope); GDPR Article 4

description: Geographic scope of the segment, expressed as ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or region identifiers (e.g., 'USA', 'GBR', 'DEU'). Defines the geographic boundary for campaign activation and regulatory compliance determination.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; GDPR Article 3; CCPA

description: Indicates whether this segment is approved for Direct-To-Consumer (DTC) marketing activation, including OTT streaming promotions, DTC merchandise offers, and subscription upsell campaigns. Supports DTC revenue strategy.
reference: Internal DTC Marketing Policy; Adobe Experience Platform Activation Governance

description: Indicates whether this segment functions as a suppression or exclusion list, identifying fans who should be excluded from specific campaigns (e.g., opted-out fans, recent purchasers, VIP do-not-contact lists). Critical for GDPR and CCPA compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 21 (Right to Object); CCPA Section 1798.120; Salesforce CRM Suppression List

description: Date and time when the segment membership was most recently evaluated and the fan_count was last updated. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Critical for assessing data freshness before campaign activation.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Segment Job API

description: Lifetime Value (LTV) classification band for fans in this segment, indicating their predicted or historical revenue contribution over their relationship with the organization. High-LTV segments receive premium personalization and VIP treatment. Supports ARPU and LTV analytics.
reference: LTV Methodology; Adobe Experience Platform Predictive Scoring; Salesforce CRM

description: Fan loyalty membership tier(s) included in this segment (e.g., 'bronze', 'silver', 'gold', 'platinum', 'vip'). Enables tier-specific personalization, exclusive offers, and loyalty program communications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bronze|silver|gold|platinum|vip|season_ticket_holder|fan_club — promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce CRM Loyalty Program Object; Internal Fan Membership Tier Taxonomy

description: Minimum propensity or confidence score from the predictive model required for fan inclusion in this segment. Expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1 (e.g., 0.7500 = 75% confidence). Applicable only when segment_type is 'predictive'.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Customer AI Scoring; Internal ML Model Governance

description: Scheduled date and time for the next segment membership evaluation run. Used by campaign operations teams to plan activation timing and ensure segment data is current before campaign launch.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Segment Scheduler

description: Net Promoter Score (NPS) classification band associated with fans in this segment: promoters (score 9-10), passives (score 7-8), detractors (score 0-6), or unknown if NPS data is unavailable. Enables NPS-driven personalization and retention campaigns.
reference: Net Promoter Score (NPS) Methodology; Adobe Experience Platform Fan Engagement Analytics

description: Name of the business unit or department responsible for this segment (e.g., 'Fan Engagement', 'Ticket Sales', 'Sponsorship Marketing', 'Merchandise', 'Broadcast DTC'). Determines accountability for segment maintenance and campaign activation.
reference: Salesforce CRM Business Unit Object; Internal Organizational Taxonomy

description: How frequently the segment membership is re-evaluated and updated. Real-time segments use Adobe Experience Platform streaming segmentation; batch segments are evaluated on a scheduled cadence. Impacts campaign freshness and system resource planning.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Streaming Segmentation; Batch Segmentation Documentation

description: RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) tier classification assigned to fans in this segment, applicable when segment_type is 'rfm'. Champions are high-value frequent buyers; lost fans have not engaged recently. Drives targeted retention and reactivation campaigns.
reference: RFM Segmentation Methodology; Salesforce CRM Fan Segmentation

description: Indicates whether this segment is scoped exclusively to season ticket holders. Season ticket holders represent the highest-value fan cohort and receive dedicated CRM treatment, renewal campaigns, and VIP experiences.
reference: Ticketmaster / AXS Season Ticket Management; Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the segment, sourced from Adobe Experience Platform audience segment key or Salesforce CRM campaign segment code. Used for cross-system referencing and campaign targeting.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Segment API; Salesforce CRM Campaign Object

description: Free-text business description of the segment's purpose, intended use case, and strategic rationale. Authored by the campaign or analytics team to provide context for downstream users and stakeholders.
reference: Salesforce CRM Campaign Description; Adobe Experience Platform Segment Metadata

description: Human-readable business name of the fan segment (e.g., 'High-Value Season Ticket Holders', 'Lapsed Casual Fans', 'Gen-Z Digital Natives'). Used in campaign targeting, reporting dashboards, and personalization workflows.
reference: Salesforce CRM Campaign Object; Adobe Experience Platform Audience

description: Current lifecycle state of the segment definition. Active segments are available for campaign targeting; draft segments are under construction; archived segments are retained for historical reference but not used in live campaigns.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Segment Lifecycle; Salesforce CRM Campaign Status

description: Classification of the segmentation methodology applied: RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary), behavioral (engagement actions), demographic (age, gender, location), psychographic (interests, values), predictive (ML-model-driven), or geographic. Drives which analytics model and personalization engine is applied.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Segmentation; Salesforce CRM Segmentation Best Practices

description: Operational system of record from which this segment definition was sourced or created. Adobe Experience Platform is the primary source for behavioral and predictive segments; Salesforce CRM is the primary source for relationship-based and campaign segments.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform; Salesforce CRM Fan and Sponsor Relationship Management

description: Secondary classification providing finer granularity within the segment type (e.g., 'churn_risk', 'upsell_candidate', 'loyalty_tier_upgrade', 'event_attendee', 'merchandise_buyer'). Enables more precise campaign targeting and personalization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: churn_risk|upsell_candidate|loyalty_tier_upgrade|event_attendee|merchandise_buyer|lapsed|reactivation — promote to reference product]
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Audience Taxonomy; Salesforce CRM Segment Hierarchy

description: Comma-separated business tags or labels applied to the segment for discoverability, governance, and categorization (e.g., 'retention, q4_campaign, nfl_season, vip, churn_prevention'). Supports segment catalog search and filtering in Adobe Experience Platform and Salesforce CRM.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform Segment Tagging; Salesforce CRM Campaign Labels

description: Date and time when this segment record was most recently modified, including changes to definition criteria, status, configuration, or metadata. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Supports change tracking and data lineage.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability)

description: Monotonically incrementing version number for the segment definition. Incremented each time the segment criteria, name, or configuration is materially changed. Supports audit trail and change management for segment governance.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; Adobe Experience Platform Segment Versioning

description: Primary key for segment_assignment

Foreign key linking to compliance.data_processing_record. Business justification: Assigning fans to segments is a personal data processing activity requiring ROPA documentation. segment_assignment has consent_basis, consent_verified, and data_suppression_flag — a DPO would expect e
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the fan profile record in the fan master data product. Identifies the individual fan being assigned to a CRM segment.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Contact / Fan Profile Object; Adobe Experience Platform — Profile Identity

description: Reference to the CRM segment definition record. Identifies which audience segment the fan has been assigned to for campaign targeting, personalization, or NPS cohort analysis.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Campaign / Segment Object; Adobe Experience Platform — Audience Segment

description: The A/B or multivariate test variant assigned to this fan within the segment for campaign experimentation. Supports controlled testing of personalization strategies, messaging, and offer effectiveness across fan cohorts.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform — A/B Test Audience Split; Salesforce CRM — Campaign Variant

description: The downstream system or platform to which this segment membership was activated (e.g., 'Adobe Target', 'Google Ads', 'Salesforce Marketing Cloud', 'Meta Ads'). Supports multi-channel activation tracking and attribution.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform — Destination Catalog; Salesforce CRM — Marketing Cloud Integration

description: Indicates whether this segment membership has been activated for downstream use in a campaign, personalization engine, or ad platform. 'activated' means the membership has been pushed to an activation destination such as Adobe Target or a paid media platform.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform — Audience Activation Status; Salesforce CRM — Campaign Member Activation

description: The date and time when this segment membership was activated and pushed to the downstream destination system. Distinct from assignment_timestamp; supports latency measurement between assignment and activation.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform — Audience Export Timestamp

description: The system or interface channel through which the assignment was executed. 'crm' = Salesforce CRM automation; 'aep' = Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) audience activation; 'api' = external API call; 'batch' = scheduled batch job; 'manual_ui' = CRM analyst via user interface.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Campaign Member Source; Adobe Experience Platform — Activation Channel

description: The calendar date on which the fan was first assigned to this segment. Used for time-variant segment membership analysis and campaign eligibility windows.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Campaign Member Start Date; Adobe Experience Platform — Segment Qualification Date

description: The mechanism by which the fan was assigned to this segment. 'rule_based' indicates automated CRM rule evaluation; 'manual' indicates a CRM analyst override; 'ml_predicted' indicates an ML model classification; 'imported' indicates a bulk data import; 'api_triggered' indicates an external system API call via Adobe Experience Platform or Salesforce API.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Segment Assignment Source; Adobe Experience Platform — Audience Qualification Method

description: Numeric score or confidence value associated with this assignment, typically produced by an ML model or rule engine. For ML-predicted assignments, this represents the model's probability or propensity score (0.0–1.0). For rule-based assignments, this may represent a weighted rule match score.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform — Audience Propensity Score; Salesforce CRM — Lead Score

description: Current lifecycle status of this fan-segment membership record. 'active' means the fan is currently a member of the segment; 'expired' means the assignment passed its expiry date; 'revoked' means it was manually removed; 'pending' means awaiting confirmation; 'suspended' means temporarily paused.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Campaign Member Status; Adobe Experience Platform — Profile Segment Membership Status

description: Precise date and time (with timezone) when the fan was assigned to this segment. Supports real-time campaign triggering and audit trail requirements.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Campaign Member Created Date; Adobe Experience Platform — Profile Segment Entry Timestamp

description: The legal basis under which the fan's data is processed for this segment assignment. Required for GDPR Article 6 compliance documentation. 'explicit_consent' = fan actively opted in; 'legitimate_interest' = business interest assessment conducted; 'contract' = necessary for service delivery; 'legal_obligation' = required by law; 'opt_out_not_exercised' = CCPA default.
reference: GDPR Article 6 — Lawful Basis for Processing; CCPA Section 1798.120 — Right to Opt-Out

description: Indicates whether the fan's data processing consent has been verified as valid and current at the time of this segment assignment. Ensures GDPR and CCPA compliance for targeted marketing use of this membership record.
reference: GDPR Article 6 — Lawfulness of Processing; CCPA Section 1798.100 — Consumer Rights

description: The date and time when this segment assignment record was first created in the data platform. Serves as the audit trail creation marker for the Silver Layer lakehouse record.
reference: SOX — Audit Trail Requirements; GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity and Accountability

description: Indicates whether this fan has exercised a right-to-suppress or opt-out request that should prevent this segment assignment from being used in outbound marketing or data sharing. Supports GDPR right to erasure and CCPA opt-out compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 17 — Right to Erasure; CCPA Section 1798.120 — Right to Opt-Out of Sale

description: The fan's overall engagement score at the time of this segment assignment, as calculated by the CRM or Adobe Experience Platform. Captured as a point-in-time snapshot to support longitudinal analysis of how engagement drives segment membership.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform — Fan Engagement Score; Salesforce CRM — Contact Engagement Score

description: The date on which this fan's membership in the segment is scheduled to expire. Null indicates an open-ended assignment with no planned expiry. Supports time-limited campaign cohorts and seasonal segment windows.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Campaign Member End Date; Adobe Experience Platform — Audience Expiry

description: The fan's account classification at the time of this segment assignment. Captured as a point-in-time snapshot to support historical cohort analysis. Values: individual consumer, family account, season ticket holder, VIP member, or fan club member.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Account Type; Ticketmaster / AXS — Account Classification

description: The geographic market or region associated with this segment assignment (e.g., 'USA', 'GBR', 'AUS'). Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes. Supports geo-targeted campaign execution and regional compliance (GDPR for EU markets, CCPA for California).
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; GDPR — EU Market Compliance; CCPA — California Market Compliance

description: Indicates whether this segment is the fan's primary or highest-priority segment assignment when a fan belongs to multiple segments simultaneously. Used for personalization and communication preference resolution.
reference: Salesforce CRM — Primary Campaign Membership; Adobe Experience Platform — Primary Audience

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Applicable Flag
description: Indicates whether this loyalty program is subject to GDPR compliance requirements due to enrollment of fans in EU jurisdictions. When True, all fan data processing under this program must comply with GDPR consent, data minimisation, and right-to-erasure requirements.

description: Indicates whether fans are automatically enrolled in this program upon meeting eligibility criteria (e.g., purchasing a season ticket or subscribing to an OTT streaming service) without requiring an explicit opt-in action. True = automatic enrollment; False = explicit opt-in required.

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary market in which this loyalty program was launched (e.g., 'USA', 'GBR', 'CAN', 'AUS'). Used for regulatory compliance scoping, market performance reporting, and multi-market program governance.

description: Cap on the number of loyalty currency units a fan can earn from a single transaction (e.g., ticket purchase, merchandise order, PPV event). Prevents abuse and controls program liability. Null if no per-transaction cap is defined.

description: Long-form marketing and operational description of the season ticket package, including included events, seat location narrative, and STH benefit highlights. Used in fan-facing communications, CRM campaigns, and the Ticketmaster/AXS product listing.
reference: Salesforce CRM; Ticketmaster / AXS

description: Human-readable marketing name of the season ticket package as displayed to fans and used in CRM communications (e.g., 'Full Season Gold Plan', 'Half-Season Flex Pack'). Sourced from Salesforce CRM product catalog.
reference: Salesforce CRM; Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management

description: Current lifecycle state of the season ticket package product definition. Active packages are available for sale or renewal; sold_out indicates inventory exhaustion; suspended indicates temporary hold; retired indicates end-of-life for the product definition.
reference: Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Classification of the season ticket package by coverage configuration. Determines the number of included events and pricing tier. Full-season covers all home games; half-season covers 50%; mini-plan covers a curated subset; flex-pack allows fan-selected games; group_plan for bulk group sales; premium_suite for luxury suite packages.
reference: Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management; Ticketmaster / AXS

description: The payment schedule configuration available for this season ticket package. Determines how the face value is collected from the STH (Season Ticket Holder) — either as a single upfront payment or spread across installment periods. Drives SAP FI accounts receivable scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; PCI-DSS; Ticketmaster / AXS

description: The date by which an existing STH (Season Ticket Holder) must exercise their renewal right for this package before it is released to the general waitlist. Drives CRM renewal campaign scheduling in Salesforce.
reference: Salesforce CRM; Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management

description: Numeric rank determining the STH (Season Ticket Holder) renewal priority order for this package tier. Lower values indicate higher priority (e.g., rank 1 = first right of renewal). Critical for managing the annual renewal window and STH retention strategy.
reference: Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management; Salesforce CRM

description: Indicates whether tickets within this season ticket package may be listed on the official resale marketplace (e.g., Ticketmaster Fan-to-Fan Exchange). Governs resale rights per league CBA (Collective Bargaining Agreement) and team policy.
reference: Ticketmaster / AXS; CBA; League Governance

description: The date on which this season ticket package is no longer available for purchase. After this date, the package status transitions to inactive or sold_out. Controls the off-sale window in Ticketmaster/AXS.
reference: Ticketmaster / AXS; Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management

description: The date on which this season ticket package becomes available for new purchase or renewal. Controls the on-sale window in Ticketmaster/AXS and Archtics inventory systems.
reference: Ticketmaster / AXS; Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management

description: The SAP S/4HANA MM material number assigned to this season ticket package as a sellable product in the ERP system. Enables financial posting, revenue recognition, and SD order management alignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/SD; SOX

description: The specific seat number within the assigned row for this season ticket package. Sourced from Archtics seating inventory. Null for GA (General Admission) or SRO (Standing Room Only) package types.
reference: Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management; ADA

description: Number of seats included in this season ticket package definition. Typically 1 for individual packages; higher values for group plans or suite packages. Drives inventory allocation in Archtics.
reference: Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management

description: The row designation within the assigned section for this season ticket package (e.g., 'A', 'B', '12'). Sourced from Archtics. Null for flex-pack or GA (General Admission) package types where specific seats are not pre-assigned.
reference: Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management

description: The venue section designation associated with this season ticket package (e.g., 'Lower Bowl', 'Club Level', 'Upper Deck'). Sourced from Archtics seating inventory and used for seat assignment and pricing tier validation.
reference: Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management; ADA

description: Number of this season ticket package units that have been sold or confirmed for the current season. Used for inventory tracking, revenue forecasting in Workday Adaptive Planning, and sold_out threshold monitoring.
reference: Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management; Workday Adaptive Planning

business_glossary_term: Season Ticket Holder (STH) Loyalty Points Awarded
description: Number of fan loyalty points awarded to the STH (Season Ticket Holder) upon purchase or renewal of this package. Feeds into the fan engagement and NPS (Net Promoter Score) program managed via Adobe Experience Platform.
reference: Adobe Experience Platform; Salesforce CRM

description: Version identifier of the legal terms and conditions governing this season ticket package (e.g., 'v2.1'). Required for GDPR, CCPA, and SOX compliance to ensure the correct contractual terms are associated with each package sold.
reference: GDPR; CCPA; SOX; CBA

description: Total number of home events (games, matches, or performances) included in this season ticket package. Defines the scope of the package and is used to calculate per-event cost for fan communications and analytics.
reference: Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management; League Governance

description: Indicates whether the STH (Season Ticket Holder) may transfer individual game tickets within this package to another person via the Ticketmaster/AXS platform. Distinct from resale eligibility — transfer is a non-commercial action.
reference: Ticketmaster / AXS; League Governance

description: The timestamp when this season ticket package record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and SOX audit trail.
reference: SOX; GDPR; Databricks Lakehouse

description: Indicates whether fans can join a waitlist for this season ticket package when it is sold out. Drives the waitlist management workflow in Salesforce CRM and Archtics inventory management.
reference: Salesforce CRM; Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management

description: Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the ticket refund record. Primary key for the refund data product in the ticketing domain Silver layer.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10

description: Foreign key linking to ticketing.access_entitlement. Business justification: When a refund is processed, the associated access_entitlement must be voided or invalidated. Linking refund → access_entitlement (access_entitlement_id) enables the system to directly identify and voi
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Refunds reduce AR balances and must be applied against specific AR invoices for AR credit memo processing and open-item clearing. Standard AR workflow in sports entertainment requires refund-to-invoic
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Refund costs are charged back to cost centers for departmental P&L accountability in sports entertainment operations. The existing cost_center_code is denormalized; a proper FK enables cost center-lev
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Refunds must be posted to the correct fiscal period for accurate P&L reporting and prior-period adjustment tracking. Finance teams reconcile refund liabilities by fiscal period during period-close; th
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to league.franchise. Business justification: Franchise-level refund liability tracking is a named financial reporting process — postponed or cancelled home games trigger refund obligations charged to the franchise. Direct franchise attribution o
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Refunds must be posted to specific GL accounts (revenue reversal, refund liability) for ERP reconciliation. The existing gl_account_code plain attribute is a denormalized reference; replacing it with
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.investigation. Business justification: Compliance-driven refunds (fraud investigations, regulatory sanctions, event cancellations under investigation) require linking refund records to the triggering investigation. refund has is_fraud_rela
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the sports or entertainment event associated with the refunded ticket. Enables event-level refund aggregation for cancellation and postponement analysis.
reference: Event Planning and Scheduling Domain

description: Reference to the fan (customer) account requesting or receiving the refund. Supports CRM-linked fan engagement tracking and PCI-DSS audit trail for cardholder refund activity.
reference: Salesforce CRM Fan Record; GDPR Article 4; CCPA

description: Reference to the fan (customer) account requesting or receiving the refund. Supports CRM-linked fan engagement tracking and PCI-DSS audit trail for cardholder refund activity.
reference: Salesforce CRM Fan Record; GDPR Article 4; CCPA

description: Reference to the sports or entertainment event associated with the refunded ticket. Enables event-level refund aggregation for cancellation and postponement analysis.
reference: Event Planning and Scheduling Domain

description: Reference to the specific ticket being refunded. A single order may contain multiple tickets; this field identifies the individual ticket entitlement subject to the refund.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Ticket Entitlement

description: Reference to the specific ticket being refunded. A single order may contain multiple tickets; this field identifies the individual ticket entitlement subject to the refund.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Ticket Entitlement

description: Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: Refunds are operationally triggered by security incidents — event suspension due to emergency activation, fan ejection leading to ticket refund, or venue evacuation. Linking refund to security_inciden
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the originating ticket order from which this refund was initiated. Links the refund back to the original purchase transaction in Ticketmaster/AXS.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Order Management

description: Foreign key linking to ticketing.ticket_order_line. Business justification: A refund is frequently issued at the line-item level (e.g., refunding a single seat within a multi-seat order). refund already has ticket_order_id (existing) for order-level traceability, but lacks a
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to ticketing.ticket_payment. Business justification: A refund must trace back to the original payment transaction for PCI-DSS compliance, financial reconciliation, and chargeback management. refund.payment_transaction_ref is a STRING external processor
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The authorization tier at which the refund was approved. AUTO indicates system-triggered approval (e.g., event cancellation policy); AGENT, SUPERVISOR, and FINANCE indicate escalating human approval tiers based on refund amount thresholds.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls; PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10

description: The date and time when the refund request was approved by an authorized agent or automated rule engine. Null if the refund has not yet been approved or was rejected. Supports approval SLA reporting.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10.2.1; SOX Section 404 Approval Controls

description: The date and time when the refund was confirmed as successfully received by the fan's payment instrument or wallet. Represents the final settled state of the refund lifecycle.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10; SAP S/4HANA FI Clearing

description: The date and time when the refund record was first created in the system of record (Ticketmaster/AXS). Audit trail field for data lineage and PCI-DSS compliance logging.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10.2; SOX Section 404

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the refund transaction (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency operations across global markets and SAP FI foreign currency revaluation.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Multi-Currency

description: The total face value of the ticket(s) being refunded before any deductions such as service fees or processing charges. Represents the base monetary amount subject to reversal in SAP FI.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Revenue Reversal; PCI DSS v4.0; SOX Section 404

description: Indicates whether the refund was triggered by an event-level action (cancellation, postponement, force majeure) rather than a fan-initiated request. Drives bulk refund processing workflows and league governance reporting.
reference: ISO 20121 Event Sustainability; League Governance (NFL/NBA/FIFA)

description: Indicates whether the refund was initiated due to a confirmed or suspected fraudulent transaction. When true, triggers PCI-DSS fraud incident reporting and Salesforce CRM fraud case creation.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10.2.1; FCC Broadcast Fraud Regulations

description: The actual monetary amount returned to the fan after deducting any non-refundable fees or processing charges from the gross refund amount. This is the amount posted to the fan's payment instrument or credit wallet.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Net Payment; PCI DSS v4.0; SOX Section 404

description: Unstructured operational notes added by customer service agents or automated systems during the refund lifecycle. May include escalation context, fan communication history, or special handling instructions. Subject to GDPR data minimisation principles.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Notes; GDPR Article 5

description: The payment instrument used in the original ticket purchase transaction. Determines eligibility for ORIGINAL_PAYMENT refund routing and informs PCI-DSS cardholder data handling requirements.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 9; Ticketmaster/AXS Payment Processing

description: The external payment processor transaction reference number for the refund settlement (e.g., Stripe charge ID, Adyen PSP reference). Used for reconciliation between Ticketmaster/AXS and the payment gateway. Never stores raw card data.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10.2; SAP S/4HANA FI Bank Reconciliation

description: The date and time when the refund was submitted to the payment processor or credit wallet system for execution. Distinct from approved_timestamp; marks the point of financial commitment.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10; SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Run

description: Standardized code classifying the business reason for the refund. EVENT_CANCELLED and EVENT_POSTPONED are league/venue-initiated; FAN_REQUEST is voluntary; FRAUD triggers PCI-DSS fraud reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EVENT_CANCELLED|EVENT_POSTPONED|FAN_REQUEST|FRAUD|DUPLICATE_PURCHASE|VENUE_ISSUE|WEATHER — promote to reference product]
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10.2.1; ISO 20121 Event Sustainability

description: Free-text narrative providing additional context for the refund reason beyond the standardized reason code. Captured by customer service agents or automated system messages in Salesforce CRM.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Notes; GDPR Article 5 (data minimisation)

description: The channel or instrument through which the refund value is returned to the fan. ORIGINAL_PAYMENT reverses to the card/account used at purchase; CREDIT_WALLET posts to the fan's digital wallet; VOUCHER issues a future-use ticket credit. Governs SAP FI posting type.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 9.4; SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Method

description: Externally visible, human-readable refund reference number issued to the fan and used in customer service communications. Formatted as RFD- followed by a 10-digit sequence. Sourced from Ticketmaster/AXS refund workflow.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Refund Reference; PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10.2

description: Current lifecycle state of the refund request within the approval and processing workflow. Drives operational queues in Ticketmaster/AXS and SAP FI posting logic.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10; SAP S/4HANA FI Refund Workflow

description: Indicates whether the refund covers the full ticket face value (FULL), a portion of the ticket value (PARTIAL), or is issued as a credit/voucher rather than a monetary return (CREDIT). Drives revenue recognition reversal logic in SAP CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Revenue Recognition; SOX Section 404

description: Free-text explanation provided when a refund request is rejected. Populated only when refund_status is 'rejected'. Required for customer service follow-up and regulatory dispute resolution under GDPR Article 12.
reference: GDPR Article 12; Salesforce CRM Case Resolution

description: The date and time when the fan or system initiated the refund request. Represents the principal business event time for the refund lifecycle. Used for SLA measurement and PCI-DSS audit trail.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10.2; GDPR Article 12 (response timelines)

description: Indicates whether the refund relates to a ticket purchased through the secondary/resale marketplace rather than the primary box office. Resale refunds may follow different policy rules and fee structures.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Resale Marketplace Policy

description: The portion of the refund attributable to service fees originally charged on the ticket purchase. May be zero if service fees are non-refundable per the event's refund policy. Tracked separately for revenue accounting in SAP CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Allocation; PCI DSS v4.0

description: Indicates whether the refund processing exceeded the defined SLA target hours. Set to true when the elapsed time between approved_timestamp and processed_timestamp exceeds sla_target_hours. Drives escalation workflows.
reference: SLA Policy; GDPR Article 12

description: The contracted or policy-defined maximum number of hours within which the refund must be processed from the point of approval. Used for SLA compliance monitoring and customer service KPI reporting.
reference: SLA Policy; GDPR Article 12 (30-day response); PCI DSS v4.0

description: The operational system of record from which the refund record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer and reconciliation across Ticketmaster/AXS, Archtics, and SAP S/4HANA.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage; SAP S/4HANA Integration

description: The native refund identifier as recorded in the originating operational system (e.g., Ticketmaster refund transaction ID, SAP FI document number). Enables cross-system reconciliation and audit trail back to the system of record.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Refund API; SAP S/4HANA FI Document Number

description: The tax component being reversed as part of the refund. Required for VAT/GST compliance reporting in applicable jurisdictions and SAP FI tax reversal postings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Management; GDPR Article 5; SOX Section 404

description: The date and time when the refund record was last modified in the system of record. Tracks state transitions through the approval workflow for audit and reconciliation purposes.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10.2; SOX Section 404

description: The unique alphanumeric voucher code issued to the fan when refund_method is VOUCHER. Redeemable against future ticket purchases in Ticketmaster/AXS. Null for non-voucher refund methods.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Voucher Management

description: The date on which the issued refund voucher expires and can no longer be redeemed. Applicable only when refund_method is VOUCHER. Null for monetary refund methods.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Voucher Policy; GDPR Article 5

description: Primary key for resale

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link
