reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Resale transactions generate platform fee revenue and revenue share amounts requiring GL account posting. Finance teams post resale platform_fee_amount and revenue_share_amount to specific GL accounts
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.investigation. Business justification: Resale fraud investigations (scalping rings, fraudulent listings, price manipulation) require linking investigation records to specific resale transactions. Sports entertainment compliance and legal t
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: League-level seasonal resale volume reporting, revenue share settlement, and anti-scalping compliance audits are named regulatory and financial processes. Season attribution on resale transactions ena
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Resale transactions must comply with specific regulatory obligations (anti-scalping laws, consumer protection statutes). resale has anti_scalping_rule_code. Linking each resale to its governing obliga
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to ticketing.access_entitlement. Business justification: A resale transaction invalidates the original access entitlement and triggers issuance of a new one. Linking resale to the original access_entitlement via original_access_entitlement_id enables full l
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

business_glossary_term: Original Ticket Order Line Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to ticketing.ticket_order_line. Business justification: A resale transaction originates from a specific ticket that was originally purchased as a line item in a ticket order. Linking resale to the original ticket_order_line via original_ticket_order_line_i
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to ticketing.price_tier. Business justification: resale has ticket_tier as a STRING denormalization but no FK to the authoritative price_tier catalog. The resale listing price and tier classification should reference price_tier for consistent tier-b
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Anti-scalping regulatory filings (UK Secondary Ticketing Act, EU consumer protection) require tracing resale transactions to their governing regulatory submission. resale has anti_scalping_compliant a
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the fan/account record of the individual who purchased or received the resale ticket. Populated upon transaction completion or transfer acceptance.
reference: Salesforce CRM; GDPR Article 4; CCPA

description: Reference to the event for which the resale ticket is valid. Used for event-level resale volume reporting and anti-scalping rule enforcement.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS; Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management

description: Reference to the fan/account record of the individual or entity listing the ticket for resale or transfer. Used for seller identity verification and anti-scalping compliance.
reference: Salesforce CRM; GDPR Article 4; CCPA

description: Reference to the event for which the resale ticket is valid. Used for event-level resale volume reporting and anti-scalping rule enforcement.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS; Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management

description: Reference to the primary market ticket record from which this resale lifecycle originated. Links the secondary market activity back to the original ticketing entitlement.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Ticketing and Access Control; Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management

description: Reference to the primary market ticket record from which this resale lifecycle originated. Links the secondary market activity back to the original ticketing entitlement.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Ticketing and Access Control; Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management

description: Reference to the season ticket package from which this resale ticket originated, if applicable. Enables season ticket holder (STH) resale volume tracking and package-level revenue share calculations.
reference: Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management; League STH Policies

description: Reference to the venue where the event is held. Used for venue-level resale volume reporting, ADA compliance validation of resale seat accessibility, and jurisdiction-specific anti-scalping law application.
reference: Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management; ADA

description: Indicates whether the resale transaction complies with applicable anti-scalping rules (e.g., price cap relative to face value, seller type restrictions, event-specific resale windows). True = compliant; False = violation flagged for review.
reference: League anti-scalping policies; Ticketmaster/AXS Marketplace Rules; applicable state/local scalping laws

description: The specific anti-scalping rule or policy code applied to evaluate this transaction (e.g., 'PRICE_CAP_150PCT', 'STH_ONLY_RESALE', 'BLACKOUT_WINDOW'). Populated when anti_scalping_compliant is False to identify the violated rule.
reference: League anti-scalping policies; Ticketmaster/AXS Marketplace Rules

description: The date and time when this resale lifecycle record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Audit trail field for data lineage and GDPR data retention compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GDPR Article 5; SOX; ISO 20121

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this record (listing_price, sale_price, original_face_value, platform_fee_amount, seller_proceeds, revenue_share_amount). Supports multi-currency global markets.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI; PCI-DSS

description: The method by which the new entitlement token was delivered to the buyer or transfer recipient: mobile_app (push to Ticketmaster/AXS app), email (email delivery), print_at_home (PDF), will_call (venue box office pickup).
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Digital Ticket Delivery Standards

description: Indicates whether the marketplace applied dynamic pricing algorithms to adjust the listing price during the active listing period. True = dynamic pricing was applied; False = static listing price maintained.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Dynamic Pricing; League Revenue Management Policies

description: Indicates whether the seller's and buyer's GDPR data processing consent has been verified prior to processing the resale transaction. Mandatory for EU-market transactions. True = consent verified; False = consent not confirmed.
reference: GDPR Article 6; CCPA; Adobe Experience Platform Fan Engagement

description: Current state of the resale record progressing through the secondary market lifecycle: listed (active on marketplace), sold (purchase completed), transferred (peer-to-peer transfer completed), expired (listing lapsed without sale), withdrawn (seller removed listing).
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Secondary Market Standards; PCI-DSS

description: The date and time when the seller created the resale listing on the marketplace platform. Represents the start of the resale lifecycle. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS; GDPR Article 5 (storage limitation)

description: The date on which the resale listing automatically expires if not sold or withdrawn. Typically set to the event date. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Marketplace Rules

description: The asking price set by the seller at the time of listing creation, in the transaction currency. Represents the gross amount the buyer is expected to pay before marketplace fees.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS; PCI-DSS; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Externally visible alphanumeric identifier assigned to the resale listing by the marketplace platform (e.g., Ticketmaster Fan-to-Fan, AXS Marketplace). Used by sellers and buyers to reference a specific listing.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Secondary Market Standards

description: Classifies the nature of the secondary market activity: resale (commercial sale at a price), gift_transfer (no-cost transfer to another fan), forward_transfer (ticket forwarded to another account), peer_to_peer (direct fan-to-fan exchange outside marketplace).
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS; Archtics; GDPR Article 4

description: The new digital access token or barcode issued to the buyer/recipient upon completion of the resale or transfer. Replaces the original ticket token and is used for venue access control via Ticketmaster/AXS or Archtics.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Access Control; Archtics; PCI-DSS

description: Free-text field for operational notes related to the resale record, such as manual override reasons, dispute notes, or compliance review comments. Not used for structured data.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS; Internal Operations

description: The digital access token or barcode of the original ticket that was invalidated upon resale or transfer. Retained for audit trail and anti-fraud verification purposes.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Access Control; Archtics; PCI-DSS

description: The primary market face value of the ticket at the time of original purchase. Used to calculate price uplift, anti-scalping cap compliance, and revenue share calculations.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS; League anti-scalping policies; SAP S/4HANA SD

description: The payment instrument used by the buyer to complete the resale purchase (e.g., credit_card, debit_card, digital_wallet, bank_transfer, marketplace_credit). Supports PCI-DSS compliance reporting and payment analytics.
reference: PCI-DSS; PCI SSC; Ticketmaster/AXS Payment Processing

description: The tokenized or masked payment transaction reference number from the payment processor for the resale purchase. Stored in compliance with PCI-DSS tokenization requirements — no raw card data is stored.
reference: PCI-DSS; PCI SSC; Ticketmaster/AXS Payment Processing

description: The fee charged by the marketplace platform (e.g., Ticketmaster, AXS) for facilitating the resale transaction. Deducted from the sale price before calculating seller proceeds.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Marketplace Fee Schedule; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO

description: The percentage by which the sale_price exceeds the original_face_value, calculated as ((sale_price - original_face_value) / original_face_value) * 100. Used for anti-scalping compliance monitoring and dynamic pricing analytics.
reference: League anti-scalping policies; Ticketmaster/AXS

description: The portion of the resale sale price allocated back to the league, team, or rights holder per the applicable revenue share agreement. Calculated based on revenue_share_rate applied to the sale price.
reference: League Revenue Share Agreements; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; CBA

description: The contractual percentage rate (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.0500 = 5%) applied to the sale price to calculate the revenue_share_amount owed to the rights holder. Sourced from the applicable league or team revenue share agreement.
reference: League Revenue Share Agreements; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; CBA

description: The final price at which the ticket was sold on the secondary market. May differ from listing_price due to dynamic pricing adjustments or negotiated changes. Populated only when lifecycle_status is 'sold'.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS; PCI-DSS; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The individual seat number within the row for the resale ticket. Sourced from Archtics. Required for access control validation and buyer-facing listing accuracy.
reference: Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management

description: The row designation within the section for the resale ticket (e.g., 'A', '12'). Sourced from Archtics. Provides granular seat location for buyer transparency and pricing validation.
reference: Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management

description: The venue section designation of the resale ticket (e.g., 'Lower Bowl', '101', 'VIP Club'). Sourced from Archtics venue seating inventory. Used for pricing tier validation and buyer-facing listing display.
reference: Archtics Venue Seating and Inventory Management

description: Net amount remitted to the seller after deducting platform fees and any applicable revenue share from the sale price. Represents the seller's actual payout for the transaction.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS; SAP S/4HANA FI; PCI-DSS

description: Classification of the seller entity: season_ticket_holder (STH reselling allocated seats), individual_fan (casual reseller), broker (licensed ticket broker), corporate_account (business account holder). Drives anti-scalping rule applicability and revenue share tiers.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS; Archtics; League anti-scalping policies

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the payment transaction (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CAD). Supports multi-currency operations across global markets and SAP S/4HANA FI foreign currency revaluation.

description: Total monetary discount applied to the payment, including promotional codes, group sales discounts, season ticket holder discounts, and sponsor-funded promotions. Used for revenue leakage analysis and promotion ROI reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Pricing Conditions; Ticketmaster/AXS Discount Management

description: The foreign exchange rate applied at the time of payment when the transaction currency differs from the organization's functional currency (USD). Used for multi-currency financial reporting and SAP S/4HANA FI currency revaluation. Null for transactions in the functional currency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Foreign Currency; IFRS 21; ISO 4217

description: Standardized decline or failure code returned by the payment processor or card network when a payment is not authorized (e.g., insufficient_funds, card_expired, do_not_honor, fraud_suspected). Used for failed payment analysis, retry logic, and fan communication. Null for successful transactions.
reference: ISO 8583 Response Codes; Visa/Mastercard Decline Code Standards; Ticketmaster/AXS Error Handling

description: Human-readable description of the payment failure reason corresponding to the failure_reason_code. Used by customer service teams in Salesforce CRM for fan outreach and dispute resolution. Null for successful transactions.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Customer Service Integration; Salesforce CRM Case Management

description: Numeric fraud risk score assigned by the payment processor's fraud detection engine or an internal fraud scoring model at the time of payment (0.00–100.00, where higher values indicate greater fraud risk). Used for real-time payment decisioning, fraud trend analysis, and PCI-DSS fraud monitoring requirements.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10.7 (Fraud Detection); Ticketmaster/AXS Fraud Prevention; Adobe Experience Platform Risk Scoring

description: The total face value of the ticket order before any discounts, promotions, or adjustments are applied. Represents the base revenue figure used in financial reporting and EBITDA calculations in SAP S/4HANA FI.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Revenue Recognition; SOX Section 302/404 Financial Controls

description: The sequential installment number for recurring or installment-based payments (e.g., 1 of 4 for a season ticket payment plan). Null for one-time payments. Used for installment plan tracking and revenue recognition scheduling.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Season Ticket Installment Plans; SAP S/4HANA FI Installment Processing

description: The total number of installments in the payment plan for season ticket or group sales installment arrangements (e.g., 4 for a quarterly payment plan). Null for one-time payments. Used alongside installment_number for plan completion tracking.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Season Ticket Installment Plans; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Indicates whether the payment was authenticated using 3D Secure (3DS) protocol (e.g., Visa Secure, Mastercard Identity Check). True if 3DS authentication was completed. Used for fraud liability shift determination and PCI-DSS compliance reporting.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 6.4; EMVCo 3DS Specification; Visa Secure / Mastercard Identity Check

description: Indicates whether this payment is part of a recurring payment arrangement, such as a season ticket installment plan or subscription-based fan membership. True for recurring charges. Drives card-on-file compliance and CBA-related payment plan management.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3.6 (Card-on-File); Ticketmaster/AXS Installment Plans

description: Masked representation of the payment card number or digital wallet identifier (e.g., last 4 digits of card, masked wallet token). Stored in PCI-DSS compliant masked format for customer service, receipt display, and dispute identification without exposing full PAN.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3.3 (Masking of PAN); PCI SSC Data Security Standard

description: The final net amount charged to the fan after applying discounts and adding service fees and taxes (gross_amount - discount_amount + service_fee_amount + tax_amount). This is the actual amount captured from the fan's payment instrument. SSOT for revenue recognition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Revenue Recognition; SOX Section 302/404; PCI DSS v4.0

description: The interface or touchpoint through which the fan submitted the payment (e.g., web browser, mobile app, venue box office, self-service kiosk, call center, third-party resale platform). Distinct from payment method type; used for channel analytics and fraud detection.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Channel Attribution; Adobe Experience Platform

description: Classification of the payment instrument used by the fan (e.g., credit card, debit card, digital wallet such as Apple Pay/Google Pay, bank transfer, gift card, cash at box office). Drives PCI-DSS scope determination and payment processing routing.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 1; ISO 20022 Payment Instrument Classification

description: Free-text notes or comments associated with the payment transaction, entered by customer service agents or system processes (e.g., manual override justification, special handling instructions for VIP fan payments, dispute context). Used in Salesforce CRM case management.
reference: Salesforce CRM Case Notes; Ticketmaster/AXS Agent Notes

description: Externally-known, human-readable payment reference number assigned by the payment processor or ticketing platform (e.g., Ticketmaster/AXS transaction reference). Used for customer-facing receipts, dispute resolution, and reconciliation with external systems.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Payment Reference; PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3

description: Current lifecycle state of the payment transaction. Tracks the workflow from authorization through capture, failure, or refund. Critical for revenue recognition, reconciliation, and PCI-DSS compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: authorized|captured|failed|refunded|voided|pending — promote to reference product if additional states are required]
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3; SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Processing

description: Indicates whether this payment record falls within the PCI-DSS cardholder data environment (CDE) scope. True for card-based payments subject to PCI-DSS controls. Used for PCI-DSS scope boundary management, audit reporting, and data classification governance.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 2.2 (Scope Definition); PCI SSC Scoping Guidelines

description: Tokenized reference assigned by the payment processor to uniquely identify this transaction within the processor's system. Replaces raw card data per PCI-DSS tokenization requirements. Used for refund initiation, dispute management, and settlement reconciliation.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3.5 (Tokenization); PCI SSC Token Service Provider Guidelines

description: Total amount refunded to the fan for this payment transaction, applicable when payment_status is 'refunded'. May be a partial or full refund. Used for revenue reversal accounting in SAP S/4HANA FI and fan satisfaction tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Memo Processing; PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3; Ticketmaster/AXS Refund Policy

description: Categorized reason for the refund when payment_status is 'refunded'. Supports refund trend analysis, event cancellation financial impact reporting, and fraud detection. Null for non-refunded transactions.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Refund Management; SAP S/4HANA FI Credit Memo

description: The precise date and time when the refund was processed and submitted to the payment processor. Null for non-refunded transactions. Used for refund SLA monitoring and fan experience reporting.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10.3; Ticketmaster/AXS Refund SLA

description: Ticketing service and convenience fees charged to the fan on top of the ticket face value (e.g., Ticketmaster facility charge, order processing fee). Reported separately for revenue transparency and regulatory compliance.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Fee Schedule; FTC Transparency in Ticketing Guidelines

description: The date on which the payment was settled by the acquiring bank and funds were transferred to the organization's account. Critical for cash flow reporting, SAP S/4HANA FI bank reconciliation, and revenue recognition timing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Bank Reconciliation; SOX Section 302; ISO 20022

description: The batch settlement reference number assigned by the payment processor or acquiring bank when the transaction is included in a settlement batch. Used for bank reconciliation, SAP S/4HANA FI bank statement matching, and financial close processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Bank Reconciliation; PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 3; ISO 20022 Payment Settlement

description: Identifies the originating operational system that generated this payment record (e.g., Ticketmaster, AXS, Archtics box office, resale marketplace). Used for data lineage tracking, system reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance in the Databricks Lakehouse.
reference: Ticketmaster/AXS Integration; Archtics Venue Seating; Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage

description: Total tax amount applied to the ticket payment, including applicable sales tax, VAT, or entertainment tax based on the venue jurisdiction. Required for tax remittance reporting and SAP S/4HANA FI tax accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Accounting; SOX Section 302; GDPR Article 6 (Lawful Basis for Processing)

description: The timestamp when this payment record was last modified (e.g., status change from authorized to captured, or refund applied). Supports change data capture, audit trails, and Silver layer incremental processing.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10.3; SOX Section 302; GDPR Article 5(2)

description: Name of the digital wallet provider used for payment when payment_method_type is digital_wallet (e.g., Apple Pay, Google Pay, PayPal, Venmo). Null for non-wallet payment methods. Supports DTC fan engagement analytics and wallet adoption reporting.
reference: PCI DSS v4.0; Ticketmaster/AXS Digital Wallet Integration

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Doping tests are administered within a specific league's anti-doping program. League-level WADA compliance reporting and anti-doping program statistics require direct league reference; doping_test has
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: In-competition doping tests are conducted within specific league seasons. Linking doping_test to season enables season-level anti-doping program reporting (tests per season, detection rates by season)
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the athlete subject to the doping test or whereabouts filing obligation.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 5; WADA ADAMS

description: Reference to the sanctioned competition event during which an in-competition test was conducted. Null for out-of-competition tests and whereabouts records.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 4; event domain

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.whereabouts_filing. Business justification: A doping test (particularly out-of-competition testing) is conducted against a specific whereabouts filing period. The doping_test has whereabouts_filing_period as a string field. Replacing this with
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

business_glossary_term: World Anti-Doping Agency Anti-Doping Administration and Management System (WADA ADAMS) Reference
description: Unique reference number assigned within the WADA Anti-Doping Administration and Management System (ADAMS) for cross-system traceability of the test or whereabouts filing.
reference: WADA ADAMS; WADA Code Article 14

description: Indicates whether the athlete has formally requested analysis of the B-sample to contest an Adverse Analytical Finding (AAF) on the A-sample.
reference: WADA Code Article 7.3; WADA ISTI

description: Outcome of the B-sample confirmatory analysis. A confirmed result corroborates the A-sample AAF; a not-confirmed result may lead to case withdrawal.
reference: WADA Code Article 7.3; WADA ISTI

business_glossary_term: Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) Case Reference
description: Reference number of any Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) appeal or arbitration proceeding arising from this doping test result.
reference: CAS Code of Sports-related Arbitration; WADA Code Article 13

description: Current integrity status of the sample chain of custody from collection through laboratory receipt. A broken chain may invalidate the test result.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 8; WADA Code Article 3

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction where the sample was collected, used for jurisdictional reporting and WADA regional authority assignment.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; WADA ISTI

description: Calendar date on which the biological sample was collected from the athlete by the Doping Control Officer (DCO). Null for whereabouts-only records.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 6

description: Descriptive name or address of the facility or venue where the sample was collected (e.g., doping control station at stadium, athlete hotel, training facility).
reference: WADA ISTI Article 6

description: Precise date and time at which the biological sample was collected, used for chain-of-custody sequencing and time-sensitive analysis windows.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 6; ISO 8601

description: Timestamp when this doping test or whereabouts filing record was first created in the compliance system, used for audit trail and data lineage.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5(2) Accountability

description: Unique identifier of the certified Doping Control Officer (DCO) who conducted the sample collection, used for chain-of-custody accountability and DCO performance auditing.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 6; WADA DCO Certification Program

description: WADA-issued accreditation number of the testing laboratory, confirming its authorization to conduct anti-doping analyses.
reference: WADA International Standard for Laboratories (ISL)

description: Identifier or name of the WADA-accredited laboratory to which the sample was dispatched for analysis.
reference: WADA International Standard for Laboratories (ISL); WADA Code Article 6

description: Cumulative count of missed or incomplete whereabouts filings within the rolling 12-month window. Three strikes within 12 months constitutes an anti-doping rule violation under WADA Code Article 2.4.
reference: WADA Code Article 2.4; WADA ISTI Annex I

description: Cumulative count of missed doping tests (failure to be present at declared whereabouts location) within the rolling 12-month window, contributing to the Article 2.4 violation threshold.
reference: WADA Code Article 2.4; WADA ISTI Annex I

description: Address or description of the athlete's regular overnight accommodation as declared in the whereabouts filing, used by DCOs to locate the athlete for unannounced testing.
reference: WADA ISTI Annex I; GDPR Article 9 (special category data)

description: Name of the prohibited substance or method detected in the sample, as listed on the WADA Prohibited List. Null for negative results or whereabouts records.
reference: WADA Prohibited List (International Standard); WADA Code Article 4

description: WADA Prohibited List category of the detected substance or method (e.g., S1 Anabolic Agents, S2 Peptide Hormones, M1 Blood Manipulation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: S0|S1|S2|S3|S4|S5|S6|S7|S8|S9|P1|M1|M2|M3 — promote to reference product]
reference: WADA Prohibited List (International Standard); WADA Code Article 4

description: Discriminator indicating whether this record represents a physical doping test event or a whereabouts filing submission. Drives which subset of fields are populated.
reference: WADA Code Article 2.4; WADA ISTI

description: Indicates the Registered Testing Pool (RTP) tier to which the athlete belongs at the time of the test or filing, determining the applicable whereabouts and testing obligations.
reference: WADA Code Article 5.5; WADA ISTI Annex I

description: Date on which the test result was formally communicated to the athlete and relevant Anti-Doping Organization (ADO), triggering any applicable provisional suspension timelines.
reference: WADA Code Article 7.4; WADA ISTI

description: Official analytical outcome of the doping test. Adverse Analytical Finding (AAF) indicates a prohibited substance detected; Atypical Finding (ATF) requires further investigation before classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: negative|adverse_analytical_finding|atypical_finding|pending|cancelled|passport_anomaly — promote to reference product]
reference: WADA Code Article 2.1; WADA ISTI Article 7

description: Unique barcode or identifier assigned to the primary (A) sample container at the point of collection, used for laboratory tracking and chain-of-custody verification.
reference: WADA ISTI Annex B; WADA Technical Document TD2021MRPL

description: Unique barcode or identifier assigned to the reserve (B) sample container, retained for confirmatory analysis if the A-sample returns an Adverse Analytical Finding (AAF).
reference: WADA ISTI Annex B; WADA Code Article 7

description: Biological matrix collected during the test. Values include urine, blood, Athlete Biological Passport (ABP) longitudinal blood sample, or dried blood spot. Null for whereabouts-only records.
reference: WADA ISTI Annex B; WADA Technical Document TD2021MRPL

description: Date on which the period of ineligibility or sanction is scheduled to end, after which the athlete may apply for reinstatement of eligibility.
reference: WADA Code Article 10.11; CAS Arbitration Rules

description: Date on which the formal period of ineligibility or sanction commenced, used to calculate eligibility reinstatement dates.
reference: WADA Code Article 10.11; CAS Arbitration Rules

description: Current disciplinary sanction status resulting from this test record. Tracks progression from provisional suspension through formal sanction or appeal outcome.
reference: WADA Code Article 10; CAS Arbitration Rules; WADA ADAMS

description: Sport and discipline classification of the athlete at the time of the test (e.g., Athletics - 100m Sprint, Football - Outfield), used for sport-specific prohibited list application.
reference: WADA Prohibited List; FIFA Anti-Doping Regulations; IOC Anti-Doping Rules

description: Externally assigned unique identifier for the doping test event, issued by the Doping Control Officer (DCO) or testing authority at the point of sample collection. Null for whereabouts-only records.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 6; WADA ADAMS

description: Classification of the doping test as in-competition (conducted during or around a sanctioned event) or out-of-competition (conducted outside of event periods), per WADA definitions.
reference: WADA Code Article 2; WADA ISTI Article 4

description: Name of the Anti-Doping Organization (ADO) or governing body that commissioned and is responsible for the test (e.g., WADA, USADA, UKAD, league-specific anti-doping body).
reference: WADA Code Article 5; WADA ISTI Article 3

description: Indicates whether a valid Therapeutic Use Exemption (TUE) was in place for the athlete at the time of the test, which may negate an Adverse Analytical Finding (AAF) for an otherwise prohibited substance.
reference: WADA International Standard for Therapeutic Use Exemptions (ISTUE); WADA Code Article 4.4

description: Address or name of the primary training facility declared in the whereabouts filing, including scheduled training hours for the filing period.
reference: WADA ISTI Annex I; GDPR Article 9

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this record, supporting audit trail requirements and change detection in the Silver layer.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5(2) Accountability

description: Compliance classification for the whereabouts filing submission. A missed or incomplete filing may count toward the three-strike threshold for an Article 2.4 anti-doping rule violation.
reference: WADA Code Article 2.4; WADA ISTI Annex I

description: Date on which the athlete submitted their whereabouts filing for the applicable quarter. Used to determine compliance with the quarterly submission deadline.
reference: WADA Code Article 2.4; WADA ISTI Annex I

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) record in the compliance system. Primary key for the doping_violation data product.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021

description: Reference to the specific doping test (sample collection event) that triggered this violation, where applicable. Null for non-analytical violations such as whereabouts failures or tampering.
reference: WADA International Standard for Testing and Investigations (ISTI) 2021

description: FK to compliance.governing_body

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Doping violations result in prize money forfeitures, contract payment adjustments, and financial sanctions requiring journal entries. Sports entertainment finance teams must record these financial imp
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to league.league. Business justification: Doping violations result in league-level sanctions and CBA compliance actions. League-level disciplinary reporting, WADA annual reporting, and integrity dashboards require direct league reference on d
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the athlete profile record against whom the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) has been issued. Links to the athlete master data product.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 2

description: Foreign key linking to league.season. Business justification: Doping violations trigger results disqualification and standings corrections for a specific season. Season-level anti-doping reporting and results management require direct season reference on doping_
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.whereabouts_filing. Business justification: A doping violation can arise directly from whereabouts failures (missed filings or missed tests reaching the 3-strike threshold under WADA rules). Linking doping_violation to the specific whereabouts_
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Result of the analysis of the A-sample (primary sample) by the WADA-accredited laboratory. An Adverse Analytical Finding (AAF) triggers the ADRV process; an Atypical Finding (ATF) requires further investigation.
reference: WADA International Standard for Laboratories (ISL) 2021; WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 2.1

description: Date on which the appeal against the hearing decision was formally filed with the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) or the relevant appellate body. Null if no appeal has been filed.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 13; CAS Code of Sports-related Arbitration

description: Current status of any appeal lodged against the hearing decision by the athlete, the anti-doping organisation (ADO), WADA, or the relevant sports federation.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 13; CAS Procedural Rules

description: Indicates whether the athlete exercised their right to request analysis of the B-sample (confirmation sample) to verify the A-sample result. True if B-sample analysis was requested.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 7.3; WADA International Standard for Laboratories (ISL) 2021

description: Result of the B-sample analysis confirming or refuting the A-sample Adverse Analytical Finding (AAF). Confirmed means the ADRV is substantiated; not_confirmed may lead to case withdrawal. Null if B-sample was not requested.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 7.3; WADA International Standard for Laboratories (ISL) 2021

description: Length of the period of ineligibility (ban) imposed on the athlete, expressed in months. Standard WADA Code sanctions are 12, 24, or 48 months depending on substance category and aggravating/mitigating factors. Zero if no ineligibility was imposed.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 10

description: Date on which the period of ineligibility (ban) is scheduled to expire, calculated from ban_start_date plus ban_duration_months. Null if the ban is indefinite or pending final determination.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 10.11

description: Date on which the period of ineligibility (ban) officially commenced. May be backdated to the date of provisional suspension or sample collection per WADA Code Article 10.11.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 10.11

business_glossary_term: Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) Decision Date
description: Date on which the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) issued its final arbitral award on the appeal. Null if CAS proceedings are ongoing or no appeal was filed.
reference: CAS Code of Sports-related Arbitration; WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 13

business_glossary_term: Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) Outcome
description: Outcome of the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) arbitral award: whether the original sanction was upheld, reduced, increased, overturned, remanded for rehearing, or the appeal was withdrawn. Null if no CAS appeal was filed.
reference: CAS Code of Sports-related Arbitration; WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 13

business_glossary_term: Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) Reference Number
description: Official Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) case reference number assigned when the violation decision is appealed to CAS arbitration (e.g., CAS 2023/A/9876). Null if no CAS appeal has been filed.
reference: CAS Code of Sports-related Arbitration; WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 13.2

description: Free-text field for compliance officers to record case-specific notes, mitigating or aggravating circumstances, legal observations, or procedural annotations relevant to the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) case management.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 10.6 (Aggravating/Mitigating Circumstances)

description: Timestamp when the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) record was first created in the compliance system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5(2) Accountability Principle

description: Date on which the hearing body issued its written decision on the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) charge, including the sanction imposed or the case dismissal.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 8.3

description: End date of the period for which competition results are disqualified. Null if disqualification is open-ended or covers all results from the start date forward.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 9

description: Start date of the period for which competition results are disqualified. Typically the date of the sample collection or the date of the non-analytical violation.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 9

description: Name of the tribunal, panel, or disciplinary body that conducted the hearing (e.g., FIFA Disciplinary Committee, NFL Anti-Doping Program Panel, WADA Independent Hearing Panel).
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 8; CAS Procedural Rules

description: Date on which the formal disciplinary hearing was conducted by the relevant anti-doping panel or tribunal to adjudicate the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) charge.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 8; CAS Procedural Rules

description: Indicates whether the sample or violation occurred during an in-competition period (True) or out-of-competition (False). Relevant for determining which prohibited substances apply under the WADA Prohibited List.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 2.1; WADA Prohibited List

description: Name of the WADA-accredited laboratory that performed the analysis of the biological sample and issued the Adverse Analytical Finding (AAF) or Atypical Finding (ATF). Null for non-analytical violations.
reference: WADA International Standard for Laboratories (ISL) 2021

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the athlete's nationality at the time of the violation. Used for jurisdictional reporting and WADA national anti-doping organisation (NADO) notification.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 14.3

description: Date on which the athlete was formally notified of the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) by the anti-doping organisation (ADO) or governing body. Triggers the provisional suspension clock under WADA Code.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 7.4

description: Description of the prohibited method used (e.g., blood doping, gene doping, manipulation of samples), where the violation involves a prohibited method rather than a substance. Null when not applicable.
reference: WADA Prohibited List (International Standard) — Methods M1–M3

description: Name of the prohibited substance or metabolite detected in the analytical finding, as identified by the WADA-accredited laboratory. Null for non-analytical violations. Aligned with the WADA Prohibited List.
reference: WADA Prohibited List (International Standard); WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 2.1

description: WADA Prohibited List category code for the detected substance or method (e.g., S1 Anabolic Agents, S2 Peptide Hormones, M1 Manipulation of Blood). Null for non-analytical violations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: S0|S1|S2|S3|S4|S5|S6|S7|S8|S9|M1|M2|M3|P1 — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: WADA Prohibited List (International Standard) — Section Codes

description: Date on which the provisional suspension of the athlete took effect, pending the outcome of the hearing process. Null if no provisional suspension was imposed.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 7.4.2

description: Date on which the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) and sanction were publicly disclosed on the governing body's or WADA's official website per mandatory publication requirements. Null if not yet disclosed or exempt from disclosure.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 14.3

description: Indicates whether the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) and sanction have been publicly disclosed per WADA Code Article 14.3 mandatory publication requirements. False if the case involves a minor athlete or is subject to confidentiality provisions.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 14.3; GDPR Article 9 (Special Category Data)

description: Date from which the athlete is eligible to apply for reinstatement and return to competition following completion of the period of ineligibility and satisfaction of all reinstatement conditions (e.g., whereabouts compliance, return-to-competition testing).
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 10.12; WADA International Standard for Results Management (ISRM)

description: Indicates whether the athlete's competition results from the relevant period have been automatically disqualified as a consequence of the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV), including forfeiture of medals, points, and prizes.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 9

description: Revised period of ineligibility in months as determined by the appellate body (CAS or other). Populated only when the appeal resulted in a modification of the original sanction. Null if no revision occurred.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 13; CAS Code of Sports-related Arbitration

description: Date on which the biological sample (urine, blood) was collected from the athlete. For analytical violations, this is the principal real-world event date. Null for non-analytical violations.
reference: WADA International Standard for Testing and Investigations (ISTI) 2021

description: Type of sanction imposed following the hearing decision. Ineligibility (ban) is the primary sanction; others include reprimand, disqualification of results, financial fine, warning, or conditional suspension.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Articles 9–10

description: The specific sport or discipline in which the athlete competes and under which the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) was adjudicated (e.g., Football, Basketball, Athletics, Cycling).
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021; FIFA Anti-Doping Regulations

description: Indicates whether the athlete held a valid Therapeutic Use Exemption (TUE) for the detected substance at the time of the sample collection. A valid TUE may result in the ADRV charge being withdrawn.
reference: WADA International Standard for Therapeutic Use Exemptions (ISTUE) 2021; WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 4.4

description: Timestamp when the Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) record was most recently modified in the compliance system. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking for regulatory reporting.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5(2) Accountability Principle

description: Externally-known unique alphanumeric reference code assigned to this Anti-Doping Rule Violation (ADRV) by the governing body or anti-doping organisation (ADO) for case tracking and correspondence.
reference: WADA World Anti-Doping Code 2021 Article 2; CAS Procedural Rules

business_glossary_term: Anti-Doping Administration and Management System (ADAMS) Reference Number
description: Unique reference number assigned by the WADA ADAMS portal upon submission of the whereabouts filing. Used as the externally-known business identifier for cross-referencing with WADA and national anti-doping organisations.
reference: WADA ADAMS System; WADA ISTI Article 11.3.2

description: Deadline by which the athlete must respond to a missed or incomplete filing notification. Typically 14 days from notification_date per WADA ISTI. Failure to respond within this window may result in the missed filing being confirmed.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.4

description: Full name of the authorised representative (agent, coach, or team official) who submitted the filing on behalf of the athlete, where applicable. Null if the athlete submitted directly.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.2

business_glossary_term: Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) Case Reference
description: Reference number of the CAS arbitration case associated with a disputed whereabouts filing violation, if applicable. Null if no CAS proceedings have been initiated. Format follows CAS standard case numbering (e.g., CAS 2024/A/9876).
reference: CAS Arbitration Rules; WADA Code Article 13

description: End date of the scheduled competition or event during the filing period. Defines the in-competition period boundary for testing jurisdiction and sample classification purposes.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.3(d)

description: Start date of the scheduled competition or event during the filing period. Used to plan in-competition doping control and to validate athlete availability windows.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.3(d)

description: Regulatory compliance determination for this filing period. COMPLIANT = filed on time and complete; MISSED = not filed by deadline; INCOMPLETE = filed but missing required information; LATE = filed after deadline; UNDER_REVIEW = compliance determination pending adjudication. Three MISSED or INCOMPLETE filings within 12 months trigger a WADA Code Article 2.4 violation.
reference: WADA Code Article 2.4; WADA ISTI Article 11.3.3

description: Date and time when this whereabouts filing record was first created in the compliance data platform. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for the Silver Layer lakehouse record.
reference: SOX Section 404; WADA ISTI Article 11

description: Origin system or ingestion method through which this whereabouts filing record was loaded into the compliance data platform. ADAMS = direct integration with WADA ADAMS portal; MANUAL_ENTRY = entered by compliance staff; API_INTEGRATION = via anti-doping organisation API; BULK_IMPORT = batch file load.
reference: WADA ADAMS System; SOX Section 404

description: Free-text description of the reason provided by the athlete or their representative for disputing a missed or incomplete filing determination. Relevant for CAS arbitration proceedings and WADA Code Article 2.4 adjudication.
reference: WADA Code Article 2.4; CAS Arbitration Rules

description: Regulatory deadline by which the athlete must submit their whereabouts filing for the applicable quarter. Typically the last day of the preceding quarter per WADA ISTI requirements.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.1

description: Free-text notes or comments recorded by the compliance officer or anti-doping administrator regarding this whereabouts filing. May include exceptional circumstances, force majeure events, or administrative observations relevant to compliance adjudication.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11; ISO 20121 Event Sustainability Management

description: Calendar quarter (1=Q1 Jan-Mar, 2=Q2 Apr-Jun, 3=Q3 Jul-Sep, 4=Q4 Oct-Dec) to which this whereabouts filing applies. Under WADA Code Article 2.4, three missed filings within 12 months constitute a whereabouts failure violation.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.1; WADA Code Article 2.4

description: Calendar year (e.g., 2024) to which this whereabouts filing applies. Combined with filing_period_quarter to uniquely identify the quarterly filing obligation period.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.1

description: Flag indicating whether the filing was submitted after the regulatory deadline (submission_date > filing_deadline). Late submissions may be treated as incomplete or missed filings depending on the anti-doping organisation's rules.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.3; WADA Code Article 2.4

description: Date and time of the most recent amendment or update to this whereabouts filing record. Supports audit trail requirements and compliance adjudication when disputes arise over filing completeness.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.3; SOX Section 404

description: Running count of missed or non-compliant whereabouts filings by this athlete within the preceding 12-month rolling window at the time of this filing. When this value reaches 3, a WADA Code Article 2.4 whereabouts failure violation is triggered. Stored as a point-in-time snapshot for audit and adjudication purposes.
reference: WADA Code Article 2.4; WADA ISTI Article 11.3.4

description: Date on which the athlete was formally notified of the missed or incomplete filing determination. The 14-day response window for the athlete to contest the determination begins from this date.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.4; WADA Code Article 2.4

description: Flag indicating whether the athlete has been formally notified of a missed or incomplete filing determination as required by WADA ISTI. Notification is a prerequisite before a missed filing can be counted toward the Article 2.4 violation threshold.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.4; WADA Code Article 2.4

description: Full street address where the athlete will be sleeping each night during the filing period. Required field under WADA ISTI for out-of-competition testing. Classified as restricted PII as it reveals the athlete's residential or accommodation location.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.3(a)

description: City component of the athlete's overnight location for the filing period. Supports geographic analysis of testing pool distribution and logistics planning for out-of-competition testing.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.3(a)

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the athlete's overnight location during the filing period. Used to coordinate testing jurisdiction between national and international anti-doping organisations.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.3(a); ISO 3166-1

description: Name of the Anti-Doping Organisation (ADO) responsible for managing this athlete's whereabouts filing obligation for the period (e.g., WADA, USADA, UKAD, or the relevant International Federation). Determines which body adjudicates missed filing violations.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.2.1; WADA Code Article 2.4

description: Current status of the compliance review process for this filing. PENDING = awaiting review; IN_REVIEW = under active compliance review; ACCEPTED = compliance determination finalised; DISPUTED = athlete or representative has contested the compliance determination; ESCALATED = referred to CAS or governing body for adjudication.
reference: WADA Code Article 2.4; CAS Arbitration Rules; WADA ISTI Article 11.3.4

description: Specific 60-minute daily time window (HH:MM in 24-hour format, representing start of slot) during which the athlete guarantees availability for out-of-competition testing at the declared training venue. This is a mandatory WADA ISTI requirement for Registered Testing Pool athletes.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.3(c)

description: Sport and discipline for which the athlete is registered in the testing pool (e.g., Athletics - 100m Sprint, Swimming - Freestyle, Football). Used for sport-specific whereabouts rule application and reporting to governing bodies such as FIFA, IOC, or relevant International Federation.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.2; IOC Anti-Doping Rules

description: Calendar date on which the athlete or their authorised representative submitted the whereabouts filing. Used to determine whether the filing was on-time or late relative to the filing_deadline.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.2

description: Channel or mechanism through which the whereabouts filing was submitted. ADAMS_PORTAL and ADAMS_MOBILE are the primary WADA-approved electronic submission methods. Other methods may be accepted in exceptional circumstances per anti-doping organisation policy.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.2

description: Precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the whereabouts filing was received and recorded in the ADAMS portal. Provides audit-grade precision for compliance adjudication.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.2; WADA Code Article 2.4

description: Role of the person who submitted the whereabouts filing on behalf of the athlete. Athletes may authorise agents, coaches, or team officials to submit on their behalf via ADAMS. Relevant for accountability and adjudication of missed filing disputes.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.2; WADA Code Article 2.4

description: Classification of the registered testing pool to which the athlete belongs. RTP = Registered Testing Pool (international-level, managed by International Federation or WADA); NRTP = National Registered Testing Pool (managed by National Anti-Doping Organisation); TP = Testing Pool (lower-tier, no whereabouts filing obligation but tracked for completeness).
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.2; WADA Code Article 2.4

description: Scheduled end time of the athlete's regular training sessions during the filing period. Together with training_schedule_start_time, defines the daily availability window for doping control officers.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.3(b)

description: Scheduled start time of the athlete's regular training sessions during the filing period. Combined with training_schedule_end_time, defines the 60-minute testing slot window required by WADA ISTI.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.3(b)

description: Full street address of the primary training venue for the filing period. Enables doping control officers to locate the athlete for unannounced out-of-competition testing.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.3(b)

description: Name of the primary training facility or venue where the athlete trains during the filing period. Required for out-of-competition testing planning and sample collection logistics.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.3(b)

description: Number of times this whereabouts filing has been updated or amended after initial submission. Athletes may update filings up to the deadline without penalty; updates after the deadline may be flagged for review.
reference: WADA ISTI Article 11.3.3

description: Flag indicating whether this filing caused the athlete's missed filing count to reach or exceed the WADA Code Article 2.4 threshold of three missed/incomplete filings within 12 months, triggering a formal whereabouts failure violation.
reference: WADA Code Article 2.4

description: Primary privacy regulation governing this request or consent record. Determines the applicable deadline, response format, and legal basis requirements. 'BOTH' applies when the data subject qualifies under both GDPR (EU resident) and CCPA (California resident). 'OTHER' covers emerging state-level privacy laws (e.g., VCDPA, CPA).
reference: GDPR; CCPA Section 1798.100; VCDPA; Colorado Privacy Act

description: Internal team responsible for processing and fulfilling this privacy request. Supports workload routing, SLA monitoring, and escalation management. Values: privacy_ops (Privacy Operations team), legal (Legal department), dpo_office (Data Protection Officer's office), fan_services (Fan Services for consent queries), it_security (IT Security for data deletion execution).
reference: GDPR Article 12; ISO/IEC 27701

description: Specific action taken on the consent record. Applicable when interaction_type = 'consent'. 'granted' = affirmative consent given; 'withdrawn' = previously granted consent revoked; 'updated' = consent scope modified; 'expired' = consent lapsed per policy version expiry. Null for rights requests.
reference: GDPR Article 7(3); CCPA Section 1798.120

description: Date and time when the data subject affirmatively granted consent. Serves as the legal basis timestamp for processing activities relying on consent under GDPR Article 6(1)(a). Null for rights requests or consent withdrawal records.
reference: GDPR Article 7; GDPR Recital 32

description: Version identifier of the privacy policy or consent notice that was presented to the data subject at the time of consent capture. Enables tracking of consent validity across policy updates and supports re-consent workflows when material policy changes occur. Format: v{major}.{minor}.
reference: GDPR Article 7; GDPR Recital 42; CCPA Section 1798.130

description: Reference identifier or URI pointing to the immutable consent proof artifact stored in the compliance document repository. Captures the exact consent interaction evidence (e.g., screenshot, API payload hash, signed consent token) required to demonstrate lawful basis under GDPR Article 7(1).
reference: GDPR Article 7(1); GDPR Recital 42

description: Category of consent being captured or withdrawn. Applicable when interaction_type = 'consent'. Values: marketing (promotional communications), analytics (behavioral and performance analytics), third_party_sharing (data sharing with sponsors/partners), cookies (web/app cookie consent), biometric_data (facial recognition or biometric identification at venues). Null for rights requests.
reference: GDPR Article 6-7; CCPA Section 1798.120; BIPA (Biometric Information Privacy Act)

description: Date and time when the data subject withdrew previously granted consent. Upon withdrawal, downstream processing activities relying on this consent must cease. Integrated with Adobe Experience Platform consent management to suppress marketing and analytics processing.
reference: GDPR Article 7(3); CCPA Section 1798.120(b)

description: Date and time when this privacy request record was first created in the compliance system. Audit trail field supporting data lineage and regulatory accountability requirements under GDPR Article 5(2).
reference: GDPR Article 5(2); ISO/IEC 27701

description: Comma-separated list of personal data categories covered by this request or consent record (e.g., 'ticketing_history, fan_profile, payment_data, location_data, biometric_data'). Derived from the data subject's request or the consent scope definition. Used for scoping the fulfillment workflow and data inventory mapping.
reference: GDPR Article 15(1)(b); CCPA Section 1798.110(c)

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the jurisdiction of the data subject's residence, used to determine the applicable privacy regulation (GDPR for EU/EEA residents, CCPA for California residents, etc.) and the corresponding response obligations. Examples: GBR, DEU, USA, CAN.
reference: GDPR Article 3; CCPA Section 1798.140; ISO 3166-1

business_glossary_term: Data Protection Officer (DPO) Review Required Flag
description: Indicates whether this request requires review by the Data Protection Officer (DPO) before fulfillment. Set to True for high-risk erasure requests, requests involving special category data (biometric, health), or requests that may conflict with legal retention obligations.
reference: GDPR Article 37-39; GDPR Article 9

description: Date and time when the Data Protection Officer (DPO) completed their review of the privacy request. Populated only when dpo_review_required = True. Supports audit trail for high-risk processing decisions.
reference: GDPR Article 37-39

description: Revised response deadline after a formal extension has been granted and the data subject notified. Replaces regulatory_deadline as the operative SLA date when an extension is invoked. Null if no extension was applied.
reference: GDPR Article 12(3); CCPA Section 1798.105(b)

description: Date and time when the data subject was notified of a response deadline extension. Under GDPR, organizations may extend the 30-day deadline by a further 60 days for complex requests, but must notify the data subject within the initial 30-day window. Under CCPA, a 45-day extension is permitted with notice.
reference: GDPR Article 12(3); CCPA Section 1798.105(b)

description: Date on which the privacy request was fully fulfilled and the response was delivered to the data subject. Used to calculate actual response time against the regulatory deadline and to confirm SLA compliance. Null until the request reaches 'fulfilled' status.
reference: GDPR Article 12(3); CCPA Section 1798.105(b)

description: Method used to verify the identity of the requestor. Supports audit trail requirements and helps assess verification strength. Values: email_confirmation (link-based), account_login (authenticated session), knowledge_based (security questions), document_upload (ID document), two_factor (MFA challenge).
reference: GDPR Article 12(6); CCPA Section 1798.130(a)(3)(B)

description: Status of the identity verification process for the requestor. 'pending' = verification initiated but not complete; 'verified' = identity confirmed; 'failed' = verification unsuccessful (request cannot proceed); 'waived' = verification not required per policy (e.g., consent withdrawal). Mandatory step before fulfilling access or erasure requests.
reference: GDPR Article 12(6); CCPA Section 1798.130(a)(3)(B)

description: Top-level discriminator indicating whether this record represents a data subject rights request (access, erasure, portability, etc.) or a consent management event (grant, withdrawal, update). Drives downstream processing workflow and regulatory deadline calculation.
reference: GDPR Articles 15-21; CCPA Section 1798.100-1798.135

description: Indicates whether the request was submitted by an authorized agent acting on behalf of the data subject rather than the data subject themselves. When True, agent authorization documentation must be collected and linked via agent_authorization_reference. Required for CCPA compliance.
reference: CCPA Section 1798.135(b); GDPR Article 17(1)

description: Indicates whether the data subject's records are subject to a legal hold that prevents erasure or restriction fulfillment. When True, the erasure or restriction request must be suspended pending resolution of the legal matter. Coordinated with the legal case management system.
reference: GDPR Article 17(3)(e); CCPA Section 1798.105(d)(6)

description: Free-text field capturing internal notes, decisions, and observations recorded by the processing team during fulfillment. Includes documentation of exemptions applied, systems searched, and any complexities encountered. Confidential — not shared with the data subject.
reference: GDPR Article 5(2) (Accountability Principle); ISO/IEC 27701

description: Externally-communicated, human-readable reference number assigned to the privacy request or consent record at submission. Used in all correspondence with the data subject and regulatory bodies. Format: PRV-{YYYY}-{8-digit sequence}.
reference: GDPR Article 12; CCPA Section 1798.105

description: Calculated deadline by which the organization must fulfill or respond to the privacy request. Derived from submitted_at plus the applicable regulatory window: 30 calendar days under GDPR (extendable to 90 with notice) or 45 calendar days under CCPA (extendable to 90). Stored as a business date for SLA monitoring and escalation triggers.
reference: GDPR Article 12(3); CCPA Section 1798.105(b)

description: Documented legal or operational basis for rejecting or partially fulfilling a privacy request. Required when response_outcome is 'rejected' or 'partially_fulfilled'. Must reference a valid legal exemption (e.g., 'legitimate interests override', 'legal obligation to retain', 'manifestly unfounded request'). Populated in the response letter to the data subject.
reference: GDPR Article 12(4); GDPR Article 17(3); CCPA Section 1798.105(d)

description: Current lifecycle state of the privacy request or consent record. Tracks progression from initial submission through identity verification, processing, and final resolution. 'escalated' indicates referral to legal or DPO for complex cases. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: submitted|verified|in_progress|fulfilled|rejected|withdrawn|escalated — promote to reference product]
reference: GDPR Article 12(3); CCPA Section 1798.105(b)

description: Specific type of data subject rights request submitted. Applicable when interaction_type = 'rights_request'. Values: access_sar (Subject Access Request), erasure (Right to be Forgotten), portability (Data Portability), rectification (Correction), restriction (Processing Restriction), opt_out_of_sale (CCPA Opt-Out of Sale/Sharing). Null for consent records.
reference: GDPR Articles 15-21; CCPA Section 1798.120

description: Email address provided by the requestor at submission. Used for identity verification correspondence and delivery of the privacy request response. Stored as PII under GDPR Article 4 and CCPA.
reference: GDPR Article 4(1); CCPA Section 1798.140(o)

description: Full legal name of the individual submitting the privacy request. May differ from the fan record name when submitted by an authorized agent or legal representative on behalf of the data subject.
reference: GDPR Article 12; CCPA Section 1798.130(a)(3)

description: Method used to deliver the privacy request response or consent confirmation to the data subject. Must align with the data subject's preferred contact channel and regulatory requirements for secure delivery of personal data.
reference: GDPR Article 12(1); CCPA Section 1798.130(a)(2)

description: Final outcome of the privacy request fulfillment. 'fulfilled' = request fully completed; 'partially_fulfilled' = some data categories actioned, others exempted; 'rejected' = request denied with legal basis; 'no_data_found' = no personal data located for the subject; 'extended' = response period extended per regulatory allowance with notice sent.
reference: GDPR Article 12(4); CCPA Section 1798.105(b)

description: Channel through which the data subject submitted the privacy request or provided/withdrew consent. Supports channel analytics and compliance audit trails. Values align with Adobe Experience Platform touchpoint taxonomy.
reference: GDPR Article 12; CCPA Section 1798.130(a)

description: Exact date and time (with timezone) when the privacy request was submitted or the consent event was recorded. This is the principal business event timestamp and the anchor for all regulatory deadline calculations (GDPR 30-day, CCPA 45-day).
reference: GDPR Article 12(3); CCPA Section 1798.105(b)

description: Comma-separated list of third-party organizations to whom the data subject's personal data has been disclosed and who must be notified of erasure, restriction, or opt-out requests. Includes sponsors, broadcast partners, and analytics vendors. Required for GDPR Article 17(2) and CCPA opt-out propagation.
reference: GDPR Article 17(2); GDPR Article 19; CCPA Section 1798.120

description: Date and time when this privacy request record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change detection for downstream integrations with Adobe Experience Platform and Salesforce CRM.
reference: GDPR Article 5(2); ISO/IEC 27701

description: Two-letter US state code for data subjects residing in the United States. Used to determine applicability of state-level privacy laws beyond CCPA (e.g., VCDPA for Virginia, CPA for Colorado, CTDPA for Connecticut). Populated only when data_subject_jurisdiction = 'USA'.
reference: CCPA; VCDPA; Colorado Privacy Act; Connecticut Data Privacy Act

description: Date and time when the requestor's identity was successfully verified. Marks the start of the regulatory response clock in jurisdictions where the deadline begins upon verification rather than submission.
reference: GDPR Article 12(3); CCPA Section 1798.105(b)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each governing body or regulatory authority record in the Sports Entertainment compliance reference master.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Widely recognized abbreviation or acronym for the governing body (e.g., FIFA, WADA, IOC, FCC, SEC, OSHA, PCI SSC).
reference: Industry Standard Nomenclature

description: Annual fee or dues payable by Sports Entertainment to this governing body for membership, certification, or regulatory registration (in the denomination of penalty_currency_code).

description: Indicates whether this governing body has anti-doping testing, adjudication, or enforcement authority over Sports Entertainment's athletes (e.g., True for WADA, sport-specific anti-doping organizations).

description: Comma-separated list of Sports Entertainment business domains subject to this governing body's authority (e.g., 'Athlete,Broadcast,Fan,Finance' for GDPR; 'Athlete' for WADA; 'Broadcast' for FCC). Drives compliance scope mapping.

description: Primary regulatory framework, statute, or standard enforced by this body (e.g., GDPR, CCPA, PCI-DSS, SOX, WADA Code, ADA Title III, ISO 20121, CBA). Used to map compliance obligations to the correct authority.

description: Frequency at which this governing body conducts or requires compliance audits, inspections, or assessments of Sports Entertainment's operations (e.g., annual for SOX/PCI-DSS, event_triggered for WADA testing, continuous for GDPR supervisory monitoring).

description: Classification of the governing body by its organizational nature: sports_federation (FIFA, IOC, NFL, NBA, MLB, NHL, MLS), regulatory_agency (FCC, SEC, OSHA, ADA), standards_body (PCI SSC, ISO, WADA), arbitration_body (CAS), government_agency, or certification_body.
