description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the company code master record. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse pipeline and SOX-compliant audit trail for financial master data changes.
reference: SOX Section 404; SAP S/4HANA Change Documents; ISO 8601

description: 20-character ISO 17442 Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) assigned by a Local Operating Unit (LOU) for publicly traded or regulated Sports Entertainment entities. Required for SEC financial reporting, derivatives reporting under Dodd-Frank, and international regulatory filings.
reference: ISO 17442; SEC Regulation; GLEIF LEI Standard; Dodd-Frank Act

description: The full registered legal name of the Sports Entertainment subsidiary or operating company as filed with the relevant government authority (e.g., 'Sports Entertainment Broadcast Network LLC', 'League Operating Company Inc.'). Used in SEC filings, SOX attestations, and intercompany agreements.
reference: SEC Regulation S-K; SOX Section 302; SAP S/4HANA FI (BUTXT)

description: Percentage of the legal entity's equity not owned by the Sports Entertainment parent, representing non-controlling interest. Used in SEC consolidated financial statements for minority interest disclosure and EBITDA attribution. Range 0.00 to 100.00.
reference: SEC Regulation S-X ASC 810; IFRS IFRS 10; SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting

description: The SAP S/4HANA company code of the immediate parent entity in the legal entity hierarchy. Enables hierarchical traversal of the Sports Entertainment corporate structure for intercompany settlement, consolidation, and segment reporting. Null for the ultimate parent holding company.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Organizational Hierarchy; SEC Regulation S-X; SOX Section 404

description: Postal or ZIP code of the registered office address. Used for tax jurisdiction determination, regulatory filings, and ADA venue accessibility compliance mapping.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Address (PSTLZ); IRS Tax Jurisdiction; ADA Compliance

description: SAP S/4HANA posting period variant assigned to this company code, controlling which fiscal periods are open or closed for financial postings. Enforces period-end close discipline and SOX-compliant financial controls by preventing backdated entries.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI (XVVWA / T001B); SOX Section 404

description: Name of the SAP S/4HANA profit center standard hierarchy node to which this company code's profit centers are assigned. Enables segment-level EBITDA reporting and management accounting roll-ups across league, broadcast, venue, and merchandise business units.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA (Profit Center Accounting); EBITDA Reporting; SEC Segment Reporting ASC 280

description: ISO 3166-2 state, province, or region code of the registered office (e.g., 'NY', 'CA', 'TX'). Used for state tax compliance, CCPA applicability determination, and regulatory jurisdiction mapping.
reference: ISO 3166-2; CCPA; SAP S/4HANA FI Address (REGIO)

business_glossary_term: Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Reporting Entity Flag
description: Indicates whether this company code is a registrant or consolidated subsidiary required to be included in SEC financial filings (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K). Drives inclusion in the consolidation perimeter for group financial reporting.
reference: SEC Regulation S-X; SEC Exchange Act Section 13; SOX Section 302

description: Abbreviated or trading name of the legal entity used in internal reporting, dashboards, and ERP display fields. Corresponds to SAP S/4HANA company code short text (KTEXT). Distinct from the full legal name.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Module (KTEXT)

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this company code master record was sourced. Primarily SAP S/4HANA FI/CO for financial master data. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking in the Databricks lakehouse.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage; SAP S/4HANA FI; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail

description: Indicates whether this company code is within scope for Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) Section 302 and 404 internal control assessments. True for all entities included in the consolidated SEC financial statements of the publicly traded Sports Entertainment parent.
reference: SOX Section 302; SOX Section 404; SEC Regulation S-X

description: Street address of the registered office or principal place of business for the legal entity. Used in SEC filings, regulatory correspondence, and legal notices. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.
reference: SEC Regulation S-K Item 102; SAP S/4HANA FI Address Management

description: Government-issued tax identification number for the legal entity (e.g., US Employer Identification Number (EIN), EU VAT registration number). Required for tax filings, vendor invoicing, and regulatory reporting. Stored as confidential business data.
reference: IRS EIN; EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; OECD BEPS; SOX Section 302

description: Value Added Tax (VAT) registration number for entities operating in VAT-applicable jurisdictions (EU, UK, etc.). Used on broadcast rights invoices, sponsorship agreements, and merchandise licensing contracts. Distinct from the domestic tax registration number.
reference: EU VAT Directive 2006/112/EC; HMRC VAT Registration; SAP S/4HANA FI Tax

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the cost center record in the Sports Entertainment lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key generated by the platform.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module — Cost Center Master Data

description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Cost centers in SAP CO belong to a specific company code (legal entity). The company_code string field is a denormalized reference that should be replaced with a typed FK to company_code.company_code_
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Cost centers are assigned to profit centers in SAP CO for segment reporting. The profit_center_code string field is a denormalized reference that should be replaced with a typed FK to profit_center.pr
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the physical venue or facility primarily associated with this cost center, where applicable (e.g., a venue operations cost center linked to a specific arena). Enables venue-level cost analysis and facility management reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; Sports Entertainment Venue Master Data

description: Primary SAP CO activity type associated with this cost center, representing the unit of output or service provided (e.g., 'BROADCAST_HR' for broadcast production hours, 'VENUE_DAY' for venue operating days). Used for activity-based cost allocation and internal service pricing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-ACT; Activity-Based Costing

description: Method used to allocate or settle costs from this cost center to other cost objects at period end: direct (direct activity allocation), assessment (using assessment cycles), distribution (using distribution cycles), activity_based (ABC costing), or statistical (for reporting only, no actual settlement).
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; IFRS 2; Activity-Based Costing Standards

description: Total approved annual budget allocated to this cost center for the current fiscal year, expressed in the cost center's primary currency. Sourced from Workday Adaptive Planning and loaded into SAP CO for variance reporting. Used in EBITDA forecasting and FP&A dashboards.
reference: Workday Adaptive Planning; SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; SOX Section 302

description: Sports Entertainment business domain that this cost center primarily supports. Enables cross-domain EBITDA analysis and cost attribution across the enterprise (e.g., 'venue' for arena operations, 'broadcast' for OTT/RSN production costs). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: venue|broadcast|athlete|league|sponsorship|merchandise|ticketing|fan_engagement|content|finance|workforce|compliance|digital_platform — promote to reference product]
reference: Sports Entertainment Enterprise Data Model; SAP S/4HANA CO-OM

description: Sports Entertainment business unit or division that owns this cost center (e.g., 'NFL Operations', 'OTT Streaming', 'Venue Management', 'Athlete Performance'). Used for segment-level EBITDA reporting and ARR tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; IFRS 8; SEC Regulation S-X

description: SAP CO controlling area to which this cost center belongs. The controlling area defines the organizational unit within which cost accounting is performed and cross-company cost allocations are managed (e.g., 'SE01' for Sports Entertainment North America).
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; IFRS 8 — Operating Segments

description: Single-character or two-character SAP CO cost center category code that controls which activity types and statistical key figures can be posted (e.g., 'A' = general, 'B' = production, 'F' = financial). Determines default allocation rules and reporting groupings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA

description: Alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the cost center within the controlling area, as defined in SAP CO. This is the externally-known business identifier used in journal entries, purchase orders, and budget allocations (e.g., 'VEN-OPS-001', 'BCAST-PROD-02').
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; SOX Section 404 — Internal Controls over Financial Reporting

description: Free-text description of the cost center's business purpose, scope of operations, and cost accountability. Provides context for financial analysts, auditors, and FP&A teams beyond what the name and code convey (e.g., 'Responsible for all operational costs of the home venue including HVAC, BMS, security, and event-day staffing').
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; SOX Section 404 — Documentation Requirements

description: Full descriptive name of the cost center as maintained in SAP CO master data (e.g., 'Venue Operations — Staples Center', 'Broadcast Production — NFL Sunday Package'). Used in financial reports, budget dashboards, and FP&A outputs.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; SOX Section 302

description: Current lifecycle status of the cost center in SAP CO: active (accepting postings), inactive (temporarily suspended), locked (blocked for all postings), pending_activation (created but not yet effective), or archived (historical, no postings allowed). Drives period-end close controls and SOX compliance checks.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; SOX Section 404 — Internal Controls

description: SAP CO cost center category classifying the nature of cost incurrence: overhead (administrative/support), production (direct event/broadcast output), service (shared services), profit (revenue-generating unit tracked as cost center), or investment (capital project support). Drives allocation methodology and EBITDA reporting treatment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; IFRS 8; EBITDA Reporting Standards

description: SAP CO cost element group defining the set of primary cost elements (GL accounts) that can be posted to this cost center. Controls which expense categories are tracked here (e.g., 'VENUE_OPEX' for venue operating expenses, 'TALENT_COSTS' for athlete/performer costs).
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; SAP FI Chart of Accounts

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the primary country where this cost center operates. Used for statutory reporting, VAT/tax jurisdiction assignment, and GDPR/CCPA data residency compliance.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR Article 44; CCPA; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Timestamp when this cost center master record was first created in SAP CO. Used for audit trail, SOX compliance documentation, and data lineage tracking in the lakehouse Silver layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; SOX Section 404; ISO 8601

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the primary transaction currency for this cost center (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Drives currency translation in SAP FI/CO and multi-currency EBITDA consolidation.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; IFRS 21 — Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: Organizational department code associated with this cost center, aligned to the SAP SuccessFactors organizational hierarchy. Enables workforce cost attribution and headcount-to-cost analysis across Sports Entertainment business units.
reference: SAP SuccessFactors HCM; SAP S/4HANA CO-OM

description: Fiscal year to which the budget amount and planning data for this cost center applies (e.g., 2024, 2025). Aligns with the Sports Entertainment fiscal calendar defined in SAP FI company code settings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; SOX Section 404; SEC Annual Report (Form 10-K)

description: SAP FI/CO functional area classifying the cost center by business function for cost-of-sales accounting and segment reporting (e.g., 'ADMIN', 'SALES', 'PROD', 'R&D'). Required for SEC income statement presentation under US GAAP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; US GAAP ASC 220; SEC Regulation S-X

description: Geographic region or market where this cost center primarily operates (e.g., 'North America', 'EMEA', 'APAC'). Supports regional cost allocation, transfer pricing compliance, and multi-market EBITDA reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; IFRS 8 — Operating Segments; OECD Transfer Pricing Guidelines

description: SAP FI GL account group associated with this cost center, defining the range of general ledger accounts used for cost postings. Ensures chart-of-accounts alignment and supports automated financial statement mapping for SEC reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; US GAAP; IFRS; SEC Regulation S-X

description: Numeric level of this cost center within the standard cost center hierarchy (e.g., 1 = enterprise, 2 = division, 3 = department, 4 = operational unit). Used for rollup reporting in Workday Adaptive Planning and SAP CO standard hierarchy.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA Standard Hierarchy; Workday Adaptive Planning

description: Indicates whether this cost center participates in intercompany cost settlements between Sports Entertainment legal entities (True = intercompany). Triggers intercompany elimination entries in consolidated financial statements and SEC reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Intercompany; IFRS 10 — Consolidated Financial Statements; SOX Section 404

description: Timestamp of the most recent change to this cost center master record in SAP CO. Supports change detection for incremental lakehouse loads, SOX audit trails, and master data governance reviews.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; SOX Section 404; ISO 8601

description: Indicates whether budget/plan postings to this cost center are currently blocked (True = locked). Prevents changes to approved budgets after the planning cycle is closed in Workday Adaptive Planning and SAP CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; Workday Adaptive Planning

description: Indicates whether actual cost postings to this cost center are currently blocked (True = locked, no postings allowed). Used during period-end close, organizational restructuring, or SOX audit freeze periods to prevent unauthorized cost entries.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; SOX Section 404

business_glossary_term: SAP Last Changed By User ID
description: SAP user ID of the person who last modified this cost center master record. Required for SOX change management controls and audit trail. Stored as the SAP logon ID.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; SOX Section 404 — Change Management Controls

description: SAP user ID of the person who created this cost center master record in SAP CO. Required for SOX segregation-of-duties controls and audit trail documentation. Stored as the SAP logon ID (not personal name).
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; SOX Section 404 — Segregation of Duties

description: SEC/IFRS 8 reportable segment to which this cost center belongs for external financial disclosure (e.g., 'LIVE_EVENTS', 'MEDIA_RIGHTS', 'MERCHANDISE'). Drives segment footnote disclosures in SEC Form 10-K and quarterly 10-Q filings.
reference: IFRS 8 — Operating Segments; US GAAP ASC 280; SEC Form 10-K

description: Abbreviated display name for the cost center used in condensed financial reports, dashboards, and SAP transaction screens where space is limited (max 20 characters per SAP CO standard).
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA

description: Operational system of record from which this cost center master record was sourced (e.g., 'SAP_S4HANA' for records originating from SAP CO, 'WORKDAY' for records originating from Workday Adaptive Planning, 'MANUAL' for manually entered records). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: Sports Entertainment Data Governance Policy; SAP S/4HANA CO; Workday Adaptive Planning

description: Indicates whether this cost center is in scope for Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) internal control testing and audit (True = SOX relevant). SOX-relevant cost centers require documented controls, segregation of duties, and periodic management certification per SOX Sections 302 and 404.
reference: SOX Sections 302 and 404; SEC Rules 13a-15 and 15d-15

description: Date from which this cost center is active and eligible to receive cost postings in SAP CO. Defines the start of the cost center's validity period for budget planning, actual cost collection, and period-end closing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; SOX Section 404

description: Date until which this cost center is active and eligible to receive cost postings. After this date, the cost center is locked for new postings. Null indicates an open-ended validity period. Used for fiscal year planning and organizational restructuring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM-CCA; SOX Section 404

description: Primary key for fiscal_period
Reference to the parent fiscal period for hierarchical period structures (e.g., a monthly period rolling up to a quarterly period). Null for top-level periods.
Self-referencing FK on fiscal_period (prior_fiscal_period_id)
The budget version or scenario applicable to this fiscal period (e.g., Original Budget, Revised Budget, Forecast).
The calendar month (1-12) in which this fiscal period starts, used for calendar-to-fiscal mapping.
The calendar quarter (1-4) in which this fiscal period starts, used for calendar-to-fiscal mapping.
The calendar year in which this fiscal period starts, used for calendar-to-fiscal mapping.
The date when this fiscal period was officially closed for transaction entry (yyyy-MM-dd). Null if not yet closed.
Timestamp when this fiscal period record was first created in the system (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).
Total number of calendar days in this fiscal period, used for pro-rata calculations and variance analysis.
The last calendar date included in this fiscal period (yyyy-MM-dd).
The fiscal month number within the fiscal year (1-12). Null for non-monthly periods.
Additional descriptive information about the fiscal period, including special characteristics or notes.
The fiscal quarter number within the fiscal year (1-4). Null for non-quarterly periods.
The fiscal week number within the fiscal year (1-52 or 1-53). Null for non-weekly periods.
The fiscal year to which this period belongs (e.g., 2024).
Indicates whether this is a special adjustment period (e.g., Period 13) used for year-end adjustments and accruals (True/False).
Indicates whether this is the current active fiscal period (True/False).
Indicates whether this period is the final period of the fiscal year, requiring year-end closing procedures (True/False).
The date when this fiscal period was locked for final reporting and audit (yyyy-MM-dd). Null if not yet locked.
Timestamp when this fiscal period record was last modified (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).
Business identifier code for the fiscal period, typically combining fiscal year and period number (e.g., FY2024-Q1, FY2024-P01).
Human-readable name of the fiscal period (e.g., Q1 2024, January 2024, FY2024 Period 1).
Sequential period number within the fiscal year, used for ordering and reporting.

Current lifecycle status of the fiscal period. Future: not yet started; Open: active for transactions; Closed: no new transactions allowed; Locked: closed and finalized for reporting; Archived: historical record only.
Classification of the fiscal period granularity (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly, weekly, or custom).
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for financial reporting in this period (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Indicates whether this fiscal period requires SEC financial reporting filings (10-Q, 10-K) for publicly traded entities (True/False).
Indicates whether this fiscal period requires SOX compliance audit procedures (True/False).
The first calendar date included in this fiscal period (yyyy-MM-dd).
Number of business working days in this fiscal period, excluding weekends and holidays, used for operational metrics.
Unique surrogate identifier for the GL account master record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts; SOX Section 404

description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: GL accounts in SAP FI are defined within a specific company code's chart of accounts. The company_code string field is a denormalized reference that should be replaced with a typed FK to company_code.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

business_glossary_term: General Ledger (GL) Account Last Changed By User
description: SAP user ID of the person who last modified this GL account master record (SKA1.AEDTM user). Required for SOX audit trail, segregation of duties compliance, and internal audit reviews. Supports detection of unauthorized changes to chart of accounts master data.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1; SOX Section 404; SOX Section 302

business_glossary_term: General Ledger (GL) Account Last Changed Date
description: Date on which this GL account master record was last modified (SKA1.LAEDA). Used for change management tracking, SOX compliance, and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer. Triggers downstream reprocessing of dependent financial reporting datasets when account attributes change.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1; SOX Section 404

business_glossary_term: General Ledger (GL) Account Created By User
description: SAP user ID of the person who created this GL account master record (SKA1.ERNAM). Required for SOX audit trail and segregation of duties (SoD) compliance. Enables identification of unauthorized account creation and supports internal audit reviews of chart of accounts governance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1; SOX Section 404; SOX Section 302

description: Date on which this GL account master record was first created in the chart of accounts (SKA1.ERDAT). Used for audit trail, SOX compliance documentation, and chart of accounts change management reporting. Supports identification of accounts created outside of standard change control processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1; SOX Section 404; ISO 20121

description: SAP account group (SKA1.KTOKS) that controls the number range and field selection for the GL account. Groups accounts by business function, e.g., revenue accounts, expense accounts, balance sheet accounts. Determines which fields are required, optional, or suppressed during account maintenance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1; GAAP ASC 230

description: Extended descriptive name of the GL account (SKAT.TXT50) providing full business context. Used in detailed financial disclosures, audit documentation, and SEC reporting narratives.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKAT; SEC Regulation S-X; SOX Section 302

description: Short descriptive name of the GL account as maintained in SAP S/4HANA (SKAT.TXT20). Used on financial reports, trial balances, and chart of accounts listings. Examples: 'Ticket Revenue', 'Broadcast Rights Income', 'Player Salary Expense', 'Naming Rights Deferred Revenue'.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKAT; GAAP ASC 230; IFRS IAS 1

description: The externally-known, human-readable GL account number as defined in the SAP S/4HANA chart of accounts (SKA1.SAKNR). Uniquely identifies the posting target within a chart of accounts. Used on all journal entries, financial statements, and cost element assignments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1; GAAP ASC 230; IFRS IAS 1

description: Current lifecycle status of the GL account master record. 'active' accounts accept postings; 'blocked' accounts are temporarily prevented from receiving new journal entries; 'marked_for_deletion' accounts are pending archival after period-end close. Controls posting eligibility across all Sports Entertainment legal entities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1/SKB1; SOX Section 404; GAAP ASC 230

description: Fundamental financial classification of the GL account indicating its role in the financial statements (SKA1.KTOKS mapped to account type). 'asset' and 'liability' and 'equity' are balance sheet accounts; 'revenue' and 'expense' are profit and loss (P&L) accounts. Drives financial statement line assignment and period-end closing behavior.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1; GAAP ASC 205; IFRS IAS 1; SOX Section 404

description: Alternative account number used for country-specific or group reporting chart of accounts mapping (SKA1.ALTKT). Enables mapping between the operational chart of accounts and a group consolidation chart of accounts or statutory reporting chart of accounts for international league entities operating under different GAAP/IFRS frameworks.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 205

description: Indicates whether this GL account is a balance sheet account (True) or a profit and loss (P&L) income statement account (False) (SKA1.XBILK). Balance sheet accounts carry forward balances at year-end; P&L accounts are closed to retained earnings. Fundamental for period-end close processing and financial statement preparation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1; GAAP ASC 205; IFRS IAS 1

description: Four-character SAP chart of accounts key (SKA1.KTOPL) to which this GL account belongs. Sports Entertainment may maintain multiple charts of accounts for different legal entity frameworks (e.g., US GAAP vs IFRS for international leagues). Determines the account structure and financial statement mapping.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1; IFRS IAS 1; GAAP ASC 205

description: Indicates whether commitment management (purchase order commitments and funds reservations) is active for this GL account. When True, purchase orders and contracts posted against this account create budget commitments visible in FP&A reporting. Relevant for player contract commitments, venue capital expenditure, and broadcast production cost commitments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; GAAP ASC 420

description: Four-character SAP Controlling area code to which this GL account's cost element is assigned (SKA1 CO integration). The controlling area defines the organizational unit within which cost and revenue accounting is performed. Enables cross-company-code cost allocation, internal order settlement, and EBITDA reporting across Sports Entertainment's multi-entity structure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; GAAP ASC 420

description: Indicates whether a cost center assignment is mandatory when posting to this GL account. When True, all journal entries must include a valid cost center (e.g., venue operations cost center, broadcast production cost center, athlete management cost center). Enforces cost allocation discipline for EBITDA reporting and FP&A variance analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; GAAP ASC 420; SOX Section 404

description: Defines the cost element category in SAP Controlling (CO) module when this GL account is also a primary cost element (SKA1.KATYP). 'primary' cost elements correspond directly to P&L GL accounts (e.g., player salary expense, venue operating cost, broadcast production cost); 'secondary' cost elements are used for internal allocations and settlements between cost centers; 'none' for balance sheet accounts not used in CO. Critical for EBITDA reporting and cost center allocation in FP&A.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; GAAP ASC 420; EBITDA reporting standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the account currency in which balances are maintained (SKB1.WAERS). Sports Entertainment operates across global markets with revenues in USD, EUR, GBP, CAD, AUD, and other currencies. Accounts may be maintained in local currency, group currency (USD), or hard currency. Drives foreign currency revaluation and translation for consolidated SEC reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKB1; ISO 4217; GAAP ASC 830; IFRS IAS 21

description: Indicates whether this GL account has been flagged for deletion from the chart of accounts (SKA1.XLOEV). Accounts flagged for deletion cannot receive new postings and are scheduled for archival after all dependent records are cleared. Supports chart of accounts rationalization and SOX control compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1; SOX Section 404

description: SAP field status group code assigned to the GL account that controls which fields are required, optional, or suppressed during document entry (SKB1.FSTAG). Ensures data completeness for postings — for example, cost center is required on expense accounts, profit center on revenue accounts, and business area on intercompany settlement accounts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKB1; SOX Section 404

description: The financial statement version item code to which this GL account is assigned for external reporting (SKA1.BILKT). Maps the account to a specific line on the income statement, balance sheet, or cash flow statement as required for SEC 10-K/10-Q filings and EBITDA reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1; SEC Regulation S-X; GAAP ASC 205; SOX Section 302

description: Business functional area to which this GL account is assigned for segment and functional reporting (SKA1.FUNC_AREA). Enables profit and loss reporting by business function such as event operations, broadcast, athlete management, venue operations, merchandise, sponsorship, and corporate. Supports SEC segment reporting requirements under GAAP ASC 280.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP ASC 280; SEC Regulation S-X

description: Indicates whether this GL account is a house bank account used for cash management and bank reconciliation (SKB1.XINTB). When True, the account is linked to a physical bank account for automated bank statement processing, cash pooling, and treasury operations. Relevant for Sports Entertainment's multi-entity cash management across leagues and venues.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-TR; GAAP ASC 230; PCI-DSS

description: Indicates whether this GL account is designated for intercompany transactions between Sports Entertainment legal entities (e.g., between the league holding company, individual team entities, broadcast subsidiaries, and venue operating companies). When True, intercompany partner must be specified on postings for automated elimination in consolidated financial statements. Required for SOX-compliant intercompany reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; GAAP ASC 810; IFRS IAS 27; SOX Section 404

description: Indicates whether automatic interest calculation is activated for this GL account (SKB1.XINTB for interest indicator). Relevant for intercompany loan accounts, deferred revenue accounts (naming rights, multi-year broadcast deals), and overdue receivable accounts where interest accrual is contractually required.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKB1; GAAP ASC 835; IFRS IAS 39

description: Indicates whether individual line items are stored and displayable for this GL account (SKB1.XKRES). When True, each posting line is retained for drill-down reporting and audit trail purposes. Required for SOX-compliant audit trails on revenue accounts (ticket revenue, broadcast rights income, sponsorship fees) and significant expense accounts (player salaries, venue operating costs).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKB1; SOX Section 404; GAAP ASC 230

description: Indicates whether open item management is activated for this GL account (SKB1.XOPVW). When True, individual line items remain open until explicitly cleared against an offsetting entry (e.g., deferred revenue accounts for naming rights, advance ticket deposits, broadcast rights prepayments). Enables aging analysis and outstanding item tracking for FP&A.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKB1; GAAP ASC 606; IFRS IFRS 15

description: Planning level code that controls how this GL account participates in financial planning and budgeting processes in SAP CO and Workday Adaptive Planning integration. Determines the granularity at which budget vs. actual variance analysis is performed — account level, cost center level, or profit center level. Supports FP&A EBITDA budgeting and ARR tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; Workday Adaptive Planning; GAAP ASC 420

description: Flag indicating whether postings to this GL account are currently blocked at the chart of accounts level (SKA1.XSPEA). When True, no journal entries can be posted to this account regardless of company code settings. Used during account restructuring, period-end close, or SOX control enforcement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1; SOX Section 404

description: Indicates whether a profit center assignment is mandatory when posting to this GL account. When True, all postings must include a valid profit center enabling segment-level profitability reporting (e.g., by league, by venue, by broadcast network). Supports SEC segment reporting under GAAP ASC 280 and internal EBITDA reporting by business unit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA EC-PCA; GAAP ASC 280; SEC Regulation S-X

description: Indicates whether this GL account serves as a reconciliation account for a subledger and, if so, which subledger type (SKA1.MITKZ). description: Date on which the fixed asset was acquired, purchased, or constructed and capitalized. Used as the start date for depreciation calculation and CAPEX reporting. Corresponds to SAP FI-AA 'Capitalization Date'.
reference: SAP FI-AA (ANLA-AKTIV); IAS 16 para 7; GAAP ASC 360-10

description: Planned annual depreciation expense for the current fiscal year as calculated by SAP FI-AA. Used in FP&A budgeting, EBITDA bridge analysis, and Workday Adaptive Planning depreciation forecasts.
reference: SAP FI-AA Depreciation Simulation; IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360; Workday Adaptive Planning

description: High-level classification distinguishing tangible assets (stadium structures, OB trucks, scoreboards), intangible assets (broadcast rights, software licenses, NIL-related IP), right-of-use assets (IFRS 16 leases), and investment property. Drives balance sheet line-item presentation.
reference: IAS 16; IAS 38; IFRS 16; GAAP ASC 360; ASC 350

description: SAP FI-AA asset class code that determines the depreciation area, useful life defaults, and GL account determination (e.g., '1000' for Buildings, '2000' for Machinery, '3000' for Intangible Assets, '4000' for Broadcast Rights). Drives CAPEX categorization and balance sheet classification.
reference: SAP FI-AA Asset Class (ANLKL); IAS 16; IAS 38; GAAP ASC 360

description: Human-readable name of the SAP asset class (e.g., 'Stadium Structures', 'Broadcast Equipment', 'OB Trucks', 'Digital Platform Infrastructure', 'Acquired Broadcast Rights', 'Scoreboards & LED Systems'). Used in financial reporting and CAPEX dashboards.
reference: SAP FI-AA Asset Class; IAS 16; IAS 38

description: Short descriptive name of the fixed asset as recorded in SAP FI-AA (e.g., 'OB Truck Unit 7', 'Broadcast Tower – East Campus', 'Stadium Roof Structure – Section A'). Used on depreciation schedules and financial statements.
reference: SAP FI-AA Asset Master (ANLA-TXT50); IAS 16

description: Physical or logical location code where the asset is deployed (e.g., venue code, broadcast facility code, data center identifier). Supports asset tracking, insurance valuation, and ADA/OSHA compliance reporting for venue assets.
reference: SAP FI-AA (ANLA-STORT); OSHA; ADA; ISO 20121

description: Human-readable description of the asset's physical location (e.g., 'Main Stadium – East Stand', 'Broadcast Tower – Roof Level 3', 'OB Truck Depot – Training Facility'). Used in physical asset verification and insurance schedules.
reference: SAP FI-AA (ANLA-STORT); OSHA; ADA

description: Externally-known SAP FI-AA asset number (main asset number) assigned at capitalization. Used as the primary business identifier on CAPEX reports, depreciation schedules, and audit trails. Format follows SAP company-code-prefixed numbering convention.
reference: SAP FI-AA Asset Master (ANLA-ANLN1); SOX Section 404

description: Current lifecycle state of the fixed asset. 'under_construction' = Assets Under Construction (AUC) not yet capitalized; 'in_service' = actively depreciated; 'idle' = temporarily out of service; 'impaired' = impairment loss recognized; 'disposed' = sold, scrapped, or retired; 'transferred' = intercompany or intra-company transfer in progress.
reference: SAP FI-AA (ANLZ-DEACT); IAS 16 para 67; SOX Section 404

description: SAP FI-AA sub-number identifying a component or addition to the main asset (e.g., a scoreboard upgrade added to the main stadium structure asset). Enables component-level depreciation tracking.
reference: SAP FI-AA Asset Master (ANLA-ANLN2)

description: For intangible assets representing acquired broadcast rights (e.g., RSN rights, OTT streaming rights, PPV event rights), the contractual expiry date of the rights. Drives amortization end date and rights renewal planning. Null for tangible assets.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 38 para 97; GAAP ASC 350; FCC Broadcast Regulation; DRM

description: Reference to the approved CAPEX budget line item or investment program code in Workday Adaptive Planning / SAP IM (Investment Management) that authorized the acquisition of this asset. Supports CAPEX vs. budget variance reporting.
reference: SAP IM Investment Program; Workday Adaptive Planning; SOX Section 404; GAAP ASC 360

description: Timestamp when the fixed asset master record was first created in SAP FI-AA. Provides the audit trail creation event required for SOX internal controls and data lineage in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: SAP FI-AA Audit Log; SOX Section 302/404; GDPR Article 5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this asset record (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency reporting for global sports entertainment operations.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP FI Currency (TCURC-WAERS); IFRS; SEC Regulation S-X

description: SAP FI-AA depreciation area identifier representing the valuation basis (e.g., '01' for book depreciation per local GAAP, '15' for tax depreciation, '30' for IFRS). Enables parallel valuation for SEC reporting and tax compliance.
reference: SAP FI-AA Depreciation Area (T090-AFABER); IAS 16; GAAP; SOX; SEC Regulation S-X

description: SAP FI-AA depreciation key/method code defining the calculation method (e.g., 'LINR' for straight-line, 'DG30' for declining balance 30%, 'MANU' for manual, 'UNIT' for units-of-production). Determines how accumulated depreciation is calculated each period.
reference: SAP FI-AA Depreciation Key (ANLB-AFASL); IAS 16 para 62; GAAP ASC 360; SOX

description: Date on which the asset was retired, sold, scrapped, or otherwise disposed of. Triggers the derecognition of the asset from the balance sheet and recognition of any gain or loss on disposal in SAP FI-AA.
reference: SAP FI-AA (ANLA-DEAKT); IAS 16 para 67; GAAP ASC 360-10-40; SOX

description: Net proceeds received upon disposal or sale of the asset. Used to calculate the gain or loss on disposal recognized in the income statement. Relevant for stadium asset sales, OB truck fleet disposals, and broadcast rights expirations.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16 para 71; GAAP ASC 360-10-40; SOX

description: Method by which the asset was disposed of. 'sale' = sold to third party; 'scrap' = physically scrapped; 'transfer' = intercompany or intra-company transfer; 'donation' = donated; 'write_off' = written off; 'insurance_claim' = disposed via insurance settlement (e.g., storm-damaged broadcast tower).
reference: SAP FI-AA Retirement Transaction; IAS 16 para 69; GAAP ASC 360-10-40

description: Cumulative impairment loss recognized on the asset where the carrying amount exceeds the recoverable amount (per IAS 36 / ASC 360). Reduces net book value. Relevant for broadcast rights, goodwill-adjacent intangibles, and underperforming venue assets.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 36; GAAP ASC 360-10-35; SOX Section 404

description: Date the asset was placed into active service and depreciation commenced. May differ from acquisition_date for assets under construction. Drives the depreciation start period in SAP FI-AA.
reference: SAP FI-AA (ANLZ-ZUJHR); IAS 16 para 55; GAAP ASC 360

description: Reference number of the insurance policy covering this asset against loss, damage, or liability. Critical for venue assets (stadium structures), broadcast infrastructure (OB trucks, towers), and high-value equipment. Supports risk management and claims processing.
reference: SAP FI-AA Insurance Data; ISO 20121; OSHA

description: Declared insured replacement value of the asset as agreed with the insurer. May differ from net book value (NBV) for high-value venue and broadcast infrastructure assets. Used for insurance adequacy reviews.
reference: SAP FI-AA Insurance Data; ISO 20121

description: Indicates whether the asset has been fully depreciated (net book value equals residual value). Fully depreciated assets still in service are flagged for management review and potential useful life extension assessment per IAS 16.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16 para 51; GAAP ASC 360; SOX

description: Indicates whether the asset is a right-of-use (ROU) asset recognized under IFRS 16 / ASC 842 (e.g., leased venue, leased OB truck, leased broadcast satellite capacity). Drives separate balance sheet presentation and lease liability linkage.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IFRS 16; GAAP ASC 842; SEC Regulation S-X

description: Timestamp of the most recent change to the fixed asset master record in SAP FI-AA. Used for incremental data loading, change detection in the Silver Layer, and SOX audit trail completeness.
reference: SAP FI-AA Change Log; SOX Section 302/404; GDPR Article 5

description: Date of the most recent physical inventory count or asset verification exercise confirming the asset's existence and condition. Required for SOX internal controls and annual audit procedures.
reference: SAP FI-AA Physical Inventory; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP

description: Current carrying amount of the fixed asset: acquisition_cost plus subsequent_acquisition_cost minus accumulated_depreciation minus impairment_loss. Reported on the balance sheet. Refreshed each period-end close from SAP FI-AA.
reference: SAP FI-AA (ANLC-KNBTR); IAS 16 para 73; GAAP ASC 360; SOX; SEC Regulation S-X

description: SAP MM purchase order number associated with the acquisition of the asset. Provides the procurement-to-capitalization audit trail required for SOX compliance and CAPEX authorization controls.
reference: SAP MM Purchase Order (EKKO-EBELN); SOX Section 404; GAAP ASC 360

description: Estimated amount the entity expects to recover from the asset at the end of its useful life (salvage value). Deducted from acquisition cost to determine the depreciable base. Reviewed annually per IAS 16.
reference: SAP FI-AA (ANLB-SCHRW); IAS 16 para 53; GAAP ASC 360

description: Cumulative upward or downward revaluation adjustment to the asset's carrying amount under the IAS 16 revaluation model (where adopted). Recorded in the revaluation surplus in equity. Applicable to stadium land and property assets.
reference: SAP FI-AA; IAS 16 para 31-42; IFRS

description: Manufacturer's serial number or unique physical identifier for the asset (e.g., OB truck chassis number, scoreboard controller serial, broadcast transmitter serial). Used for physical verification, warranty claims, and insurance schedules.
reference: SAP FI-AA (ANLA-SERNR); OSHA; Insurance Schedule

description: Cumulative cost of subsequent capital additions or improvements to the asset (e.g., stadium seat upgrades, scoreboard technology refresh, broadcast tower antenna replacement). Added to the asset's gross book value.
reference: SAP FI-AA (ANLC-KANSW subsequent postings); IAS 16 para 10; GAAP ASC 360

description: Estimated useful life expressed in fiscal periods (months) for assets with sub-annual depreciation granularity. Used in SAP FI-AA period-based depreciation calculations alongside useful_life_years.
reference: SAP FI-AA (ANLB-NDPER); IAS 16; GAAP ASC 360

description: Estimated useful economic life of the asset in years, as determined at capitalization and used to calculate straight-line or other depreciation. Reviewed annually per IAS 16 (e.g., 40 years for stadium structures, 5 years for OB trucks, 3 years for broadcast rights).
reference: SAP FI-AA (ANLB-NDJAR); IAS 16 para 50; GAAP ASC 360; SOX

description: SAP PS Work Breakdown Structure element linked to the asset, used for CAPEX project tracking during the Assets Under Construction (AUC) phase (e.g., stadium renovation project, broadcast tower installation). Populated until the AUC is settled to a final asset.
reference: SAP PS WBS Element (PRPS-POSID); GAAP ASC 360; SOX

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each revenue recognition schedule record within the finance domain. Primary key for the revenue_recognition_schedule data product.
reference: ASC 606, IFRS 15

description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Revenue recognition schedules are scoped to a specific legal entity for SEC reporting compliance. The company_code string field should be replaced with a typed FK to company_code.company_code_id.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to event.competition_round. Business justification: Broadcast rights contracts often specify per-round fee tranches (e.g., quarterfinal, semifinal, final). Revenue recognition schedules must reference the competition round as the performance obligation
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Revenue recognition schedules are attributed to cost centers for cost allocation and reporting. The cost_center_code string field should be replaced with a typed FK to cost_center.cost_center_id.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Revenue recognition schedules recognize revenue into specific fiscal periods. Normalizes fiscal_year and fiscal_period INT columns by linking to fiscal_period master.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to event.fixture. Business justification: Under ASC 606/IFRS 15, each fixture is a performance obligation for broadcast rights and gate revenue. Revenue recognition schedules must reference the specific fixture whose completion triggers defer
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: Each recognition event generates a journal entry posting. The journal_entry_reference string field is a denormalized reference that should be replaced with a typed FK journal_entry_id → journal_entry.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to event.match_result. Business justification: Match result confirmation is the triggering event for recognizing gate receipts, PPV, and broadcast revenue. Linking revenue_recognition_schedule to match_result enables finance to confirm performance
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to broadcast.media_rights_deal. Business justification: Media rights deals are the primary contracts requiring ASC 606/IFRS 15 revenue recognition schedules. Finance creates recognition schedules per rights deal for deferred revenue management and period r
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to athlete.nil_agreement. Business justification: NIL agreement revenue must be recognized over the agreement term per ASC 606. The revenue_recognition_schedule must reference the nil_agreement driving the recognition schedule for proper deferred rev
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue recognition schedules are attributed to profit centers for segment-level revenue reporting. The profit_center_code string field should be replaced with a typed FK to profit_center.profit_cente
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue recognition schedules post recognized revenue to a specific GL account. The gl_account_code string field should be replaced with a typed FK recognized_gl_account_id → gl_account.gl_account_id.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to event.venue_assignment. Business justification: Venue revenue-sharing agreements (venue_assignment.revenue_share_pct) create deferred revenue obligations recognized over the event lifecycle. Finance must link recognition schedules to the specific v
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The revenue recognition accounting standard applied to this schedule. ASC_606 for US GAAP entities; IFRS_15 for international entities; ASC_606_IFRS_15 for dual-reporting entities. Determines the specific recognition criteria and disclosure requirements.
reference: ASC 606 (FASB), IFRS 15 (IASB), SEC Regulation S-X

description: Indicates whether the variable consideration constraint has been applied to limit the recognized amount to the extent it is highly probable (IFRS 15) or probable (ASC 606) that a significant revenue reversal will not occur. Required for ASC 606 / IFRS 15 compliance documentation.
reference: ASC 606 Step 3 — Constraining Estimates, IFRS 15.56–15.58

description: The date the underlying contract became legally binding and enforceable. Used as the ASC 606 contract inception date for determining transaction price and allocating to performance obligations.
reference: ASC 606 Step 1, IFRS 15.9, SAP S/4HANA SD

description: The date the underlying contract expires or terminates. For multi-year broadcast rights or naming rights agreements, this may be several years in the future. Nullable for evergreen contracts.
reference: ASC 606, IFRS 15, SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Classification of the underlying revenue contract driving this recognition schedule. Determines applicable recognition methodology and disclosure requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broadcast_rights|sponsorship|naming_rights|season_ticket|multi_event|licensing|merchandise|ppv|ott_subscription|nil_agreement — promote to reference product]
reference: ASC 606 Step 1, IFRS 15, SEC Regulation S-X

description: Timestamp when this revenue recognition schedule record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail creation point required for SOX compliance and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SOX Section 404, SAP S/4HANA FI Audit Trail

description: Total revenue recognized to date against this performance obligation since the recognition start date. Equals total_contract_value minus deferred_revenue_balance at any point in time. Used for period-end reconciliation and ASC 606 cumulative catch-up adjustment calculations.
reference: ASC 606, IFRS 15.B1, SAP S/4HANA RAR

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this recognition schedule (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency broadcast rights and international sponsorship contracts.
reference: ISO 4217, IFRS 15, ASC 606

description: Revenue amount to be recognized in the current accounting period for this performance obligation. Drives the period-end journal entry generation in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO. For straight-line method, equals total_contract_value divided by total periods; for milestone/event-based, equals the value of the milestone achieved.
reference: ASC 606 Step 5, IFRS 15, SAP S/4HANA FI Journal Entry

description: Current outstanding deferred revenue balance for this performance obligation — the portion of total contract value received or billed but not yet recognized as earned revenue. Drives the deferred revenue waterfall report and balance sheet liability disclosure. Updated each period-end close in SAP S/4HANA FI.
reference: ASC 606, IFRS 15, SEC Regulation S-X, SAP S/4HANA FI

business_glossary_term: Deferred Revenue General Ledger (GL) Account Code
description: SAP S/4HANA GL account code for the deferred revenue liability balance sheet account. The deferred revenue balance is carried here until recognized. Required for balance sheet reporting and SOX-compliant financial controls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts, ASC 606, SOX Section 404, SEC Regulation S-X

description: The discount rate (as a decimal, e.g., 0.0450 for 4.50%) used to adjust for the significant financing component in the transaction price. Reflects the rate that would be used in a separate financing transaction between the entity and the counterparty at contract inception. Null when significant_financing_flag is false.
reference: ASC 606-10-32-15, IFRS 15.64, SAP S/4HANA RAR

description: The date of the most recent revenue recognition posting for this performance obligation. Used to confirm period-end close completion and to identify schedules that have not been processed in the current period.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI, ASC 606, SOX

description: The effective date of the most recent contract modification affecting this recognition schedule. Null when modification_flag is false. Used to determine the prospective or cumulative catch-up adjustment period.
reference: ASC 606-10-25-12, IFRS 15.18, SAP S/4HANA SD

description: Indicates whether this recognition schedule reflects a contract modification (e.g., renegotiated broadcast rights fee, amended sponsorship scope). When true, the modification accounting treatment (prospective or cumulative catch-up) per ASC 606-10-25-12 / IFRS 15.18 must be documented.

The scheduled date for the next revenue recognition posting. Used by the FP&A team in Workday Adaptive Planning for revenue forecasting and by the accounting team to manage period-end close calendars.
reference: Workday Adaptive Planning, SAP S/4HANA RAR, ASC 606

description: Narrative description of the distinct performance obligation being recognized, as documented in the contract and required for ASC 606 / IFRS 15 disclosure. Examples: 'Exclusive broadcast rights for NFL regular season games FY2024-FY2026', 'Naming rights for stadium facade and digital signage', 'Season ticket package — 41 home games'.
reference: ASC 606 Step 2, IFRS 15.22, SEC Regulation S-K

description: Number of accounting periods for which revenue has been recognized to date against this performance obligation. Used to track progress toward full recognition and to validate cumulative_recognized_amount in period-end reconciliation.
reference: ASC 606, IFRS 15, SAP S/4HANA RAR

description: Indicates whether revenue is recognized over time (e.g., broadcast rights delivered continuously over a season) or at a point in time (e.g., a one-time PPV event, a single concert). Fundamental ASC 606 / IFRS 15 classification that determines the recognition pattern.
reference: ASC 606 Step 5, IFRS 15.31–15.38

description: The date on which revenue recognition is expected to be fully completed for this performance obligation. For multi-year broadcast rights, this may span several fiscal years. Nullable for open-ended or usage-based obligations without a fixed end date.
reference: ASC 606, IFRS 15, SAP S/4HANA RAR

description: The accounting method used to recognize revenue over the performance obligation period. Straight-line = equal amounts per period; Milestone = upon achievement of defined deliverables; Usage-based = proportional to actual consumption (e.g., broadcast impressions, CPM); Event-based = upon occurrence of specific events (e.g., game played); Proportional performance = based on percentage of obligation fulfilled.
reference: ASC 606 Step 5 — Recognize Revenue, IFRS 15.39–15.45

description: The date on which revenue recognition commences for this performance obligation. Typically aligns with contract effective date or the date the entity begins satisfying the performance obligation (e.g., first broadcast date, season opener, naming rights activation).
reference: ASC 606, IFRS 15.31, SAP S/4HANA RAR

description: Current lifecycle state of the revenue recognition schedule. Draft = under review; Active = currently recognizing revenue; Suspended = temporarily halted (e.g., contract dispute); Completed = fully recognized; Cancelled = contract terminated before full recognition.
reference: ASC 606, IFRS 15, SAP S/4HANA RAR

description: Externally-visible business identifier for this recognition schedule, formatted as RRS-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Used in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO journal entry references, audit workpapers, and SEC financial reporting disclosures.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR), ASC 606

description: Indicates whether the contract contains a significant financing component requiring adjustment of the transaction price for the time value of money per ASC 606-10-32-15 / IFRS 15.60. Common in multi-year prepaid broadcast rights deals where cash is received significantly in advance of performance.

The total transaction price allocated to this performance obligation as determined at contract inception per ASC 606 Step 3 (Determine Transaction Price) and Step 4 (Allocate Transaction Price). Expressed in the contract currency. Used as the basis for deferred revenue waterfall reporting.
reference: ASC 606 Steps 3–4, IFRS 15.73–15.86, SAP S/4HANA RAR

description: Total number of accounting periods over which revenue is to be recognized for this performance obligation. Used for straight-line recognition calculations and deferred revenue waterfall projections. For event-based recognition, represents the total number of qualifying events.
reference: ASC 606, IFRS 15, Workday Adaptive Planning FP&A

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this revenue recognition schedule record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Required for change data capture in the Databricks Silver Layer and SOX audit trail completeness.
reference: SOX Section 404, SAP S/4HANA FI Audit Trail, Databricks Delta Lake

description: The estimated amount of variable consideration included in the transaction price at the most likely amount or expected value method per ASC 606. Applicable for contracts with performance bonuses, ratings escalators, or PPV revenue share components. Null when variable_consideration_flag is false.
reference: ASC 606 Step 3, IFRS 15.53, SAP S/4HANA RAR

description: Indicates whether the transaction price includes variable consideration components (e.g., performance bonuses, ratings-based escalators, PPV revenue share, usage-based royalties). When true, the constraint on variable consideration per ASC 606 Step 3 must be applied and reassessed each reporting period.
reference: ASC 606 Step 3 — Variable Consideration, IFRS 15.50–15.59

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the accounts payable invoice record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key generated by the lakehouse ingestion pipeline from SAP FI-AP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; SOX Section 404

description: Foreign key linking to athlete.agent. Business justification: AP invoices are issued to agents for commission payments on athlete contracts. Finance teams need agent reference on AP invoices for commission payment processing, 1099 tax reporting, and agent paymen
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: AP invoices are posted within a specific legal entity (company code) in SAP FI. The company_code string field is a denormalized reference that should be replaced with a typed FK to company_code.compan
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the SAP Controlling cost center to which the invoice expense is allocated. Drives EBITDA reporting and FP&A cost attribution across business units (e.g., venue operations, broadcast, athlete management).
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-Cost Center Accounting (CSKS); SOX Section 404

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: AP invoices are posted to fiscal periods for financial reporting. Normalizes fiscal_year and fiscal_period INT columns by linking to fiscal_period master table.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: AP invoices for capital expenditures (CAPEX) are directly linked to fixed asset acquisitions in SAP FI-AA. When a vendor invoice is for a stadium renovation, broadcast equipment purchase, or other cap
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the general ledger account in the chart of accounts to which this invoice is posted. Determines financial statement line classification for SEC reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL (SKA1); SEC Regulation S-X; SOX Section 302

description: Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry. Business justification: In SAP FI, every AP invoice posting generates a corresponding journal entry (FI document). ap_invoice has sap_document_number (STRING) which references the SAP document but there is no structured FK t
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to athlete.nil_agreement. Business justification: AP invoices are generated for NIL agreement payments to athletes or their agents. The ap_invoice must reference the nil_agreement it settles for payment reconciliation, royalty tracking, and NIL compl
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to event.officiating_assignment. Business justification: Officiating assignments carry fee obligations (officiating_fee_amount, expense_allowance_amount). AP invoices for official payments must reference the specific assignment for three-way match validatio
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to event.participant. Business justification: Event participants (athletes, performers) with appearance fees (participant.appearance_fee_amount) generate AP invoices for payment. Finance must link AP invoices to the specific participant engagemen
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: SAP automatic payment program run identifier (F110) that processed this invoice for payment. Links the invoice to the payment batch for reconciliation and audit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP F110 (REGUH-LAUFD/LAUFI); SOX Section 404

description: Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: ap_invoice has profit_center_code (STRING) but no FK to finance.profit_center. In SAP FI/CO, every AP invoice is assigned to a profit center for P&L reporting. Adding profit_center_id normalizes this
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to security.security_incident. Business justification: AP invoices for security incident costs (medical services, law enforcement overtime, property repair, legal fees, third-party security contractors) must be linked to the triggering incident for insura
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Current status of the invoice in the AP approval workflow. Tracks whether the invoice has been reviewed and authorized by the appropriate cost center owner or finance controller per SOX segregation of duties requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Workflow; SOX Section 302; SOX Section 404

description: Date and time when the invoice was approved for payment in the AP workflow. Supports SLA monitoring for invoice processing cycle time and SOX audit trail.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Workflow; SOX Section 404

description: The reference date from which payment terms are calculated in SAP FI-AP. May differ from invoice date or receipt date depending on payment terms configuration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (BSEG-ZFBDT); SOX Section 404

description: Timestamp when the AP invoice record was first created in SAP FI-AP. Serves as the audit trail anchor for SOX compliance and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI (BKPF-CPUDT/CPUTM); SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code of the invoice. Sports Entertainment operates globally (USD, EUR, GBP, AUD, CAD, JPY, etc.) requiring multi-currency AP processing.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (RBKP-WAERS); IFRS 21

description: The cash discount amount available if payment is made within the early payment discount period as defined in the payment terms. Used for working capital optimization and DPO management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (BSEG-WSKTO); IAS 7

description: The calculated date by which payment must be made to the vendor based on payment terms. Used for cash flow forecasting, DPO (Days Payable Outstanding) reporting, and payment run scheduling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (BSEG-ZFBDT); IAS 7 Cash Flow Statements

description: The exchange rate applied to convert the invoice currency to the company code local currency at the time of posting. Sourced from SAP FI exchange rate tables.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI (BKPF-KURSF); IFRS 21

description: Financial classification of the invoice as capital expenditure (CAPEX), operating expenditure (OPEX), prepaid expense, accrual, or intercompany charge. Drives balance sheet vs. P&L treatment and EBITDA impact.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-CO; IFRS 16; SEC Regulation S-X; SOX Section 302

description: The SAP material document number for the goods receipt or service entry sheet that confirms delivery of goods/services from the vendor. Required for three-way match and accrual reversal.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-Goods Movement (MKPF-MBLNR); SOX Section 404

description: The total gross amount of the invoice before deduction of discounts or credits, as stated on the vendor document. Expressed in the invoice currency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (RBKP-RMWWR); IFRS 15; SOX Section 302

description: SAP CO internal order number for project-based or event-specific cost tracking (e.g., a specific concert, championship event, or venue renovation). Enables event-level P&L and ROI analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-Internal Orders (AUFK-AUFNR); SOX Section 404

description: The date printed on the vendor's invoice document. Used as the baseline for payment terms calculation and aging analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (RBKP-BLDAT); IAS 37 Provisions

description: Current workflow status of the invoice in the AP processing lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RECEIVED|UNDER_REVIEW|APPROVED|POSTED|SCHEDULED|PAID|BLOCKED|CANCELLED — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP; SOX Section 404

description: Classification of the invoice document type. STANDARD: regular vendor invoice; CREDIT_MEMO: vendor credit note; DEBIT_MEMO: debit adjustment; RECURRING: periodic service invoice; PREPAYMENT: advance payment request; INTERCOMPANY: intra-group settlement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP (RBKP-BLART); IFRS 15

description: Indicates whether this invoice is part of a recurring payment schedule (e.g., monthly venue lease, broadcast rights licensing fees, HVAC maintenance contracts). Supports automated accrual and cash flow forecasting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Recurring Documents; IFRS 16; SOX Section 404

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the AP invoice record in SAP FI-AP or the Silver Layer. Required for incremental data pipeline processing and SOX change audit trail.
