description: Four-character company code of the intercompany trading partner entity when this is an intercompany journal entry. Used for automated intercompany elimination during group consolidation. Null for external transactions. Corresponds to BKPF.VBUND.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI — BKPF.VBUND; IFRS 10; GAAP ASC 810

description: Gross total amount of the journal entry in the transaction currency. Represents the sum of all debit line items (which must equal the sum of all credit line items for a balanced entry). Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET. Corresponds to the aggregate of BSEG.WRBTR across line items.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI — BSEG.WRBTR; GAAP ASC 230; IFRS IAS 1

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each payment transaction record in the Sports Entertainment finance domain. Primary key for the payment data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Document

description: Foreign key linking to athlete.agent. Business justification: Payment records for agent commission disbursements must reference the agent being paid. Enables agent payment history tracking, commission reconciliation, and tax withholding compliance (1099/W-9) — a
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the primary invoice (AR or AP) that this payment clears or partially clears. Links inbound customer receipts to AR invoices and outbound vendor disbursements to AP invoices for cash application and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Clearing Document

description: Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: The payment table handles both AP payments (invoice_id → ap_invoice already exists) and AR receipts (customer payments against AR invoices). Adding ar_invoice_id FK enables proper linkage of incoming
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Payments are executed within a specific legal entity (company code). The company_code string field should be replaced with a typed FK to company_code.company_code_id.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the SAP CO cost center to which this payment is allocated for internal management accounting and EBITDA reporting. Supports expense allocation across event operations, venue management, broadcast, and athlete management divisions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center (CSKS)

description: Reference to the customer master record for inbound AR receipts. Identifies the payer for ticket sales, sponsorship receipts, merchandise revenue, and broadcast rights settlements. Null for outbound vendor disbursements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Customer Master (KNA1)

description: Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Payments are posted to fiscal periods for cash accounting and reconciliation. Normalizes fiscal_year and fiscal_period INT columns.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Payments post to specific GL accounts (bank clearing accounts, cash accounts). The gl_account_code string field should be replaced with a typed FK to gl_account.gl_account_id.
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Foreign key linking to fan.membership. Business justification: Membership fee payments (season tickets, VIP, fan club dues) must be reconciled against the specific membership record. Finance payment processing requires this link for cash application, auto-renewal
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Identifier for the SAP FI automatic payment program run (F110) that generated this payment. Groups all payments executed in a single batch payment run for reconciliation and audit. Null for manually posted payments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Automatic Payment Program (F110)

description: FK to finance.profit_center

Foreign key linking to ticketing.ticket_payment. Business justification: finance.payment (ERP payment record) must be reconciled with ticketing.ticket_payment (payment processor record) for cash reconciliation and period-end close. Linking these enables automated three-way
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: The NACHA ACH trace number assigned to this payment entry for ACH transactions. Used for ACH return processing, NOC (Notification of Change) handling, and bank reconciliation. Null for non-ACH payment methods.
reference: NACHA ACH Operating Rules; SAP S/4HANA FI ACH Payment Processing

description: The SAP FI house bank key (HBKID) identifying the financial institution through which this payment is routed. Used for bank reconciliation, liquidity planning, and treasury management reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI House Bank (T012.HBKID); NACHA; SWIFT

description: SAP FI payment block code (ZLSPR) indicating whether the payment is blocked from processing and the reason (e.g., manual block, dispute, audit hold, compliance review). Blank/null indicates no block. Critical for AP payment control and SOX compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Block (BSEG.ZLSPR); SOX Section 404

description: The SAP FI business area (GSBER) representing the organizational segment (e.g., ticketing, broadcast, sponsorship, merchandise, venue) for cross-company-code financial reporting and internal balance sheet preparation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Business Area (BKPF.GSBER); GAAP; IFRS 8

description: The physical or electronic check number assigned to this payment when payment_method is 'check'. Used for check register management, positive pay file submission to banks, and stop-payment processing. Null for non-check payment methods.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Check Management (PAYR.CHECF); PCI-DSS v4.0

description: The SAP FI clearing document number (AUGBL) generated when this payment clears one or more open items in the AR or AP subledger. Used for cash application matching and open item management in the general ledger.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Open Item Clearing (BSEG.AUGBL)

description: The timestamp when this payment record was first created in the SAP FI system or ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Provides the audit trail creation point required for SOX Section 404 internal controls and GDPR data lineage.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5(2); SAP S/4HANA FI Document Header (BKPF.CPUDT/CPUTM)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transaction currency in which the payment is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Essential for multi-currency operations across global sports markets. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Currency (BKPF.WAERS); IFRS 21

description: Indicates whether the payment is an outbound disbursement (AP vendor payment) or an inbound receipt (AR customer collection). Fundamental discriminator for cash flow classification and treasury reporting under IAS 7.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI AP/AR; IAS 7 Statement of Cash Flows; GAAP ASC 230

description: The cash discount amount deducted from the gross payment when early payment terms are honored (e.g., 2/10 net 30). Corresponds to BSEG.SKFBT in SAP FI. Used for discount capture analytics and vendor relationship management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Cash Discount (BSEG.SKFBT); GAAP ASC 310

description: The date on the original payment instrument (check date, wire instruction date, or bank statement date). Corresponds to BLDAT in SAP BKPF. Used for instrument-level audit and bank reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Date (BKPF.BLDAT)

description: The externally-known SAP FI payment document number assigned at the time of posting. Serves as the primary business identifier for the payment transaction used in reconciliation, audit trails, and SOX financial controls. Corresponds to BELNR in SAP BKPF.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Document (BKPF.BELNR); SOX Section 404

description: The contractual due date by which the payment obligation must be settled per invoice payment terms. Used for early payment discount eligibility, late payment penalty calculation, and days-payable-outstanding (DPO) / days-sales-outstanding (DSO) analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Net Due Date (BSEG.FAEDT); GAAP ASC 310

description: The foreign currency exchange rate applied to convert the transaction currency amount to the company code local currency (typically USD) at the time of payment posting. Sourced from SAP FI exchange rate tables (TCURR). Critical for multi-currency reconciliation and FX gain/loss reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Exchange Rate (BKPF.KURSF); IFRS 21; GAAP ASC 830

description: The total gross amount of the payment before any deductions, discounts, or withholding taxes. Expressed in the transaction currency. Forms the basis for EBITDA reporting and revenue recognition in SAP FI. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this transaction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Amount (BSEG.WRBTR); GAAP ASC 606; IFRS 15

description: The timestamp of the most recent modification to this payment record, including status changes, reversals, or data corrections. Required for SOX audit trail, change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse pipeline, and GDPR accountability.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5(2); SAP S/4HANA FI Change Documents

description: The payment amount converted to the company code local currency (USD) using the exchange_rate at posting date. Used for consolidated financial reporting, SEC filings, and EBITDA calculations in the functional currency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Local Currency Amount (BSEG.DMBTR); IFRS 21; SEC Regulation S-X

description: The net amount actually transferred after deducting cash discounts and withholding taxes from the gross amount (gross_amount - discount_amount - withholding_tax_amount). Represents the actual cash movement for treasury and bank reconciliation. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Net Amount; IAS 7; GAAP ASC 230

description: The principal business event date on which the payment was executed or value-dated by the bank. Used for cash flow reporting, aging analysis, and period-end close. Corresponds to ZFBDT (baseline date) or VALUT (value date) in SAP FI. Distinct from the document posting date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Date (BSEG.ZFBDT); IAS 7 Statement of Cash Flows

description: The financial instrument or mechanism used to execute the payment. ACH = Automated Clearing House electronic transfer; wire = bank wire transfer (SWIFT/SEPA); check = paper or electronic check; card = credit/debit card (PCI-DSS governed); digital_wallet = PayPal, Apple Pay, or similar; direct_debit = pre-authorized bank debit. Drives bank file format selection in SAP F110.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Method (BSEG.ZLSCH); PCI-DSS v4.0; NACHA ACH Rules

description: Current lifecycle state of the payment transaction within the SAP FI workflow. 'initiated' = payment instruction created; 'posted' = document posted to general ledger; 'cleared' = open items cleared; 'reversed' = payment reversed with reversal document; 'failed' = bank rejection or processing error; 'on_hold' = payment blocked pending approval or dispute resolution.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Status; SOX Section 404

description: Business classification of the payment transaction purpose. vendor_disbursement = AP payment to supplier/contractor; customer_receipt = AR collection from ticket buyer, sponsor, or broadcaster; intercompany_settlement = payment between Sports Entertainment legal entities; payroll_disbursement = athlete or staff salary payment; tax_payment = remittance to tax authority; refund = return of funds to customer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vendor_disbursement|customer_receipt|intercompany_settlement|payroll_disbursement|tax_payment|refund — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; SOX Section 404; SEC Financial Reporting

description: The accounting period posting date on which the payment document was recorded in the SAP FI general ledger. Determines the fiscal period for revenue recognition and financial reporting. May differ from payment_date for cross-period transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Date (BKPF.BUDAT); GAAP ASC 230; IAS 7

description: Free-form reference text transmitted with the payment to the beneficiary bank (e.g., invoice number, contract reference, event name). Appears on bank statements and remittance advice. Corresponds to BVTYP/XBLNR in SAP FI. Critical for counterparty cash application.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reference Document Number (BKPF.XBLNR); SWIFT MT103

description: Indicates whether a remittance advice notification has been sent to the payee or payer detailing the invoices cleared by this payment. Supports vendor and customer communication workflows in SAP FI output management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Output Management; NACHA Remittance Advice

description: The SAP FI document number of the reversal posting if this payment has been reversed (e.g., returned ACH, stopped check, duplicate payment correction). Null if the payment has not been reversed. Links to the reversing document for audit trail.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Reversal (BKPF.STBLG); SOX Section 404

description: SAP FI reversal reason code (STGRD) explaining why the payment was reversed (e.g., wrong amount, duplicate, bank return, stop payment). Null if not reversed. Used for exception reporting and fraud detection analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reversal Reason (T041T); SOX Section 404

description: SAP FI special GL indicator (UMSKZ) classifying non-standard postings such as down payments, guarantees, or security deposits associated with this payment. Blank for standard payment postings. Used for balance sheet sub-classification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Special GL Transactions (BSEG.UMSKZ); GAAP ASC 210

description: The SWIFT end-to-end transaction reference (UETR or TRN) for wire transfer payments. Enables real-time payment tracking via SWIFT gpi and bank confirmation matching. Null for non-wire payment methods.
reference: SWIFT MT103/MX pacs.008; SAP S/4HANA FI Bank Communication Management

description: The SAP FI payment terms key (ZTERM) governing the due date calculation and early payment discount conditions for this transaction (e.g., NT30, 2/10NET30). Inherited from the invoice or vendor/customer master. Drives DSO and DPO analytics.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Terms (T052.ZTERM); GAAP ASC 310

description: The amount of withholding tax deducted from the payment at source, applicable to athlete NIL payments, foreign vendor disbursements, and royalty settlements. Reported to tax authorities and reflected in SAP FI withholding tax tables (WITH_ITEM).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Withholding Tax (WITH_ITEM); IRS Form 1099/1042-S; GDPR; SOX

description: Primary key for payment_run
FK to finance.company_code

Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: payment_run has fiscal_period (INT) and fiscal_year (INT) as raw denormalized columns but no FK to finance.fiscal_period. Payment runs are executed within specific fiscal periods for period-end close
reference: Internal - LLM Generated Cross-Domain Link

description: Reference to the original payment run being reversed. Populated only when is_reversal is true. Used to maintain audit trail of corrections.

The approval workflow status for the payment run: pending indicates awaiting authorization, approved indicates cleared for execution, rejected indicates denied by approver, bypassed indicates no approval required per policy.
The date and time when the payment run was approved. Used for audit trails and compliance reporting.
The precise date and time when the payment run execution finished, whether successfully or with errors. Used to calculate processing duration and SLA compliance.
The date and time when the payment run record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage.
The total cash discount amount taken across all payments in this run for early payment. Represents savings achieved through prompt payment programs.
Detailed error messages and exception logs for failed payments or processing issues. Used for troubleshooting and root cause analysis.
The precise date and time when the payment run execution began. Critical for audit trails and reconciliation with banking systems.
The sum of all failed payment amounts that were not disbursed. Represents funds that remain in the company account and require reprocessing.
The number of payment transactions that failed during processing due to validation errors, insufficient funds, or system issues. Requires manual intervention.
Boolean flag indicating whether this payment run is a reversal of a previous run. True indicates reversal, false indicates original payment run.
The date and time when the payment run record was last modified. Used for audit trails and change tracking.
Free-text notes and comments about the payment run. Used to document special circumstances, manual interventions, or business context.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all payments in this run. All disbursements within a single run are typically in the same currency.

The electronic file format standard used for bank transmission: ISO 20022 for international standard, NACHA for US ACH, BAI2 for bank reporting, MT940 for SWIFT, CAMT.053 for account statements, proprietary for bank-specific formats.
The name of the electronic payment file generated for bank transmission. Used for file tracking and reconciliation with bank acknowledgments.

The payment instrument used for disbursements in this run: ACH for Automated Clearing House, wire for wire transfer, check for physical checks, EFT for Electronic Funds Transfer, SEPA for Single Euro Payments Area, BACS for UK Bankers Automated Clearing Services.

Current lifecycle status of the payment run: draft indicates initial creation, scheduled indicates approved and queued, processing indicates active execution, completed indicates successful finish, failed indicates execution error, cancelled indicates user-terminated run.
The standard payment terms code applied to invoices included in this run. Defines due date calculation and discount eligibility.
The accounting date on which payment transactions are posted to the general ledger. May differ from value date for period-end processing.

The status of bank statement reconciliation for this payment run: pending indicates awaiting bank confirmation, reconciled indicates matched with bank statement, discrepancy indicates mismatch requiring investigation, under_review indicates active reconciliation process.
Business-facing unique identifier for the payment run, typically formatted as PR-YYYYMMDD-NNNN for external reference and audit trails.

Classification of the payment run based on business purpose: standard for regular scheduled runs, emergency for urgent off-cycle payments, supplemental for additional payments, reversal for payment corrections, adjustment for reconciliation runs, interim for partial disbursements.
The date on which the payment run is scheduled to execute. Used for planning and cash flow forecasting.
The sum of all successfully processed payment amounts. Used to reconcile actual cash outflows against planned disbursements.
The number of payment transactions that were successfully processed and transmitted to the bank. Used to calculate success rate and identify exceptions.
The sum of all payment amounts included in this run, expressed in the payment currency. Used for cash position management and bank account reconciliation.
The total number of individual payment transactions included in this payment run. Used for reconciliation and control totals.
The date and time when the payment file was transmitted to the bank. Critical for cut-off time compliance and value dating.
The date on which funds are to be debited from the company account and credited to payee accounts. Used for cash flow forecasting and liquidity management.
The total withholding tax amount deducted from payments in this run. Required for tax compliance and reporting to tax authorities.
